sentences.zh: 永平十二年，其國王柳貌相率內屬，以其地置永昌郡也。

translations.literal: In Yongping 12 their king Liu Mao led them to submit; his territory was made Yongchang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 12 their ruler Liu Mao brought them in; the court set up Yongchang commandery on their land.

sentences.zh: 注[一九]春王猶左傳云「春王正月」也。

translations.literal: Note [nineteen]: Chun wang is as the Zuo Tradition says "the spring, the king first month."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [19]: chun wang echoes the Zuo phrase "spring, the king first month."

translations.literal: San chao means New Year day.
translations.idiomatic: The triple court falls on New Year day.

translations.literal: Chao is read zhi-yao fan.
translations.idiomatic: Chao is read zhi-yao fan.

sentences.zh: 謂歲之朝，月之朝，日之朝。

translations.literal: It means the court of the year, the court of the month, the court of the day.
translations.idiomatic: That is the morning of the year, of the month, and of the day together.

sentences.zh: 前書谷永曰：「今年正月朔，*[日]*食於三朝之會。

translations.literal: The Former Documents, Gu Yong says: "This year, first day of the first month, at the conjunction of the triple court an eclipse *[of the sun]* occurred.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong in the Han shu reports an eclipse on New Year morning at the triple audience."

translations.literal: " The Zhou li says: "A timely audience is called hui; a great gaze is called tong.
translations.idiomatic: " The Zhou li distinguishes a timed meeting as hui and a general assembly as tong."

translations.literal: " Jia Kui commentary on the Guoyu says: "Ying means to receive.
translations.idiomatic: " Jia Kui on the Guo yu glosses ying as to take in."

translations.literal: " The Odes says "therefore the hundred Man."
translations.idiomatic: " The Odes line "thereby the hundred Man" is echoed here."

translations.literal: Note [twenty]: Gong zhang means supplying and setting up curtains and hangings.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [20]: gong zhang is the provision of draped canopies.

translations.literal: Gong is read jiu-yong fan.
translations.idiomatic: Gong is read jiu-yong fan.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「三輔長無供帳之勞。

translations.literal: The Former Documents says: "The three metropolitan prefects were spared the toil of supplying hangings.
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu praises officials freed from the labor of staging canopies."

sentences.zh: 」戴延之記曰：「端門東有崇賢門，次外有雲龍門。

translations.literal: " Dai Yanzhi record says: "East of the Duan Gate is Chongxian Gate; next outside is Yunlong Gate.
translations.idiomatic: Dai Yanzhi notes that east of the Vermilion Gate stands Chongxian, and beyond it the Cloud-and-Dragon gate."

translations.literal: " Zan means to lead.
translations.idiomatic: " Zan means to conduct or usher."

sentences.zh: 注[二一]庭實，貢獻之物也。

translations.idiomatic: Commentary [21]: ting shi are the tribute objects laid in the courtyard.

sentences.zh: 左傳孟獻子曰：「臣聞聘而獻物，於是有庭實旅百。

translations.literal: Meng Xianzi in the Zuo Tradition says: "I have heard that on a friendly mission one presents goods; then there are a hundred kinds of courtyard tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Meng Xianzi in the Zuo zhuan says embassies bring gifts until the court yard overflows."

translations.literal: " "A thousand kinds" speaks of abundance.
translations.idiomatic: " A thousand kinds is rhetorical plenty."

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: "Zhong is a vessel.
translations.idiomatic: The Shuowen calls the bell a vessel."

translations.literal: " The Kong Congzi says: "Yao drank a thousand zhong.
translations.idiomatic: " The Kong Congzi credits Yao with a thousand goblets."

translations.literal: " Lei is a wine vessel.
translations.idiomatic: " Lei is a ritual wine jar."

translations.literal: The Odes says: "I will pour from that golden lei.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes line runs: "I pour you wine from the bronze lei."

translations.literal: " Zhen means the eight delicacies.
translations.idiomatic: " Zhen names the eight choice meats."

translations.literal: Tai lao is ox, sheep, and pig.
translations.idiomatic: The great sacrifice combines ox, sheep, and swine.

translations.literal: Xiang — for rhyme read xiang (fragrant).
translations.idiomatic: Xiang is read xiang for the rhyme.

translations.idiomatic: Commentary [22]: when the meal is served

translations.literal: (wei)
translations.idiomatic: (as)

translations.literal: **means* music should accompany the serving.
translations.idiomatic: **it means** music accompanies each course.

sentences.zh: 蔡邕禮樂志曰：「大予樂郊祀陵廟殿中諸食舉樂也。

translations.literal: Cai Yong Treatise on Rites and Music says: "The Da Yu music for suburban shrines, mausoleum temples, and palace banquets includes the music for each course.
translations.idiomatic: Cai Yong records that Da Yu music covers suburban, tomb, and hall feasts, with a tune for every course."

translations.literal: " Yong is the name of an ode.
translations.idiomatic: " Yong is an ode in the Classic of Poetry."

translations.literal: It means when the meal is finished they sing Yong to clear the tables.
translations.idiomatic: When diners finish they sing Yong while the tables are cleared.

translations.literal: The Analects says: "The three families used Yong at the clearing.
translations.idiomatic: The Analects rebukes three houses for singing Yong at the clearing."

sentences.zh: 」太師，樂官也，周禮，太師掌六律、六呂，以合陰陽之聲也。

translations.literal: " The grand master of music is the music officer; in the Zhou li the grand master grasps the six pitch-pipes and six female notes to harmonize yin and yang sounds.
translations.idiomatic: " The grand director of music matches the twelve pitches to yin and yang."

translations.literal: Keng is read ku-geng fan.
translations.idiomatic: Keng: fanqie ku-geng.

translations.literal: Qiang is read chu-geng fan.
translations.idiomatic: Qiang: fanqie chu-geng.

translations.literal: Yeye yuyu describes a splendid appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Yeye yuyu paints blazing splendor.

translations.literal: Yu is read yu (nurture).
translations.idiomatic: Yu is read like the word for nurture.

sentences.zh: 注[二三]左傳晏子曰：「五聲六律。

translations.idiomatic: Commentary [23]: Yan Ying lists the five notes and six pipes."

translations.literal: " Du Yu commentary says: "The five tones are gong, shang, jue, zhi, yu.
translations.idiomatic: " Du Yu names gong, shang, jue, zhi, yu."

sentences.zh: 六律，黃鐘、太蔟、姑洗、蕤賓、夷則、無射。

translations.literal: The six pitch-pipes are Huangzhong, Taicu, Guxian, Ruibin, Yize, Wuyi.
translations.idiomatic: The six male pipes are Huangzhong through Wuyi.

translations.literal: " The Documents says: "The nine tasks are in order; the nine orders are sung.
translations.idiomatic: " The Shang shu says the nine labors line up and become song."

sentences.zh: 」九功謂金、木、水、火、土、谷、正德、利用、厚生也。

translations.literal: " The nine tasks mean metal, wood, water, fire, earth, grain, upright virtue, useful work, and thickening life.
translations.idiomatic: " The nine labors are the elements of state and livelihood."

translations.literal: Yi means rows of dancers.
translations.idiomatic: Yi counts the files of dancers.

translations.literal: The Guliang Commentary says: "The Son of Heaven has eight yi.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang zhuan allows the king eight files."

translations.literal: " Shao is the name of Shun music.
translations.idiomatic: " Shao is Shun court music."

translations.literal: Wu is the name of King Wu music.
translations.idiomatic: Wu is the martial hymn of King Wu.

translations.literal: Tai gu means high antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Tai gu is farthest antiquity.

translations.literal: Note [twenty-four]: Jian means alternate.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [24]: jian means to take turns.

translations.literal: Read gu-xian fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie gu-xian.

sentences.zh: 詩國風曰「漢廣」，德廣所及也。

translations.literal: The state airs have "Han Guang" — where virtue wide reaches.
translations.idiomatic: The air Han Guang illustrates how far virtue spreads.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注周禮云：「四夷之樂，東方曰韎，南方曰任，西方曰株離，北方曰禁。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan commentary on the Zhou li says: "The music of the four Yi: east is mei, south is ren, west is zhuli, north is jin.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan names the four barbarian orchestras east to north."

translations.literal: " "Jin" in the character book is written "qian," read qu-jin fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Lexicons write the northern name as qian, read qu-jin."

translations.literal: Shu is read mo-ge fan.
translations.idiomatic: Shu: fanqie mo-ge.

sentences.zh: 周禮「仱」作「禁」，「樹」作「韎」，「兜」作「株」也。

translations.literal: The Zhou li writes "qian" as "jin," "shu" as "mei," "dou" as "zhu."
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou li swaps several of these graphs for variant spellings.

sentences.zh: 注[二五]萬樂、百禮，盛言之也。

translations.literal: Note [twenty-five]: "Ten thousand tunes" and "hundred rites" are said for emphasis.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [25]: "myriad music" and "hundred rites" are hyperbole.

translations.literal: Ji means to arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Ji means reaching completion.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「天地絪縕，萬物化醇。

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Heaven and earth intertwine; the myriad things transform and mature.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says heaven and earth mingle and things ripen."

sentences.zh: 」禮統曰：「天地者，元氣之所生，萬物之祖。

translations.literal: " The Li tong says: "Heaven and earth are where primal vapor is born — the ancestor of the myriad things.
translations.idiomatic: " The Li tong calls heaven and earth the womb of primal breath."

sentences.zh: 」尚書大傳曰：「天子將入，撞蕤賓之鐘，左五鐘皆應。

translations.literal: " The Great Tradition on the Documents says: "When the Son of Heaven is about to enter he strikes the Ruibin bell, and the five bells to the left all respond.
translations.idiomatic: " The Shang shu da zhuan says the returning sovereign strikes Ruibin and five bells answer."

translations.literal: " Zhuang is read zhi-jiang fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Zhuang: fanqie zhi-jiang."

translations.literal: Thereupon the sage sovereign *
translations.idiomatic: Then the sage Son of Heaven

translations.literal: (qin)
translations.idiomatic: (in person)

sentences.zh: **[鶯]*萬方之歡娛，久沐浴乎膏澤，懼其侈心之將萌，而怠於東作也，[一]乃申舊章，下明詔，命有司，班憲度，昭節儉，示大素。

translations.literal: **shared* the myriad regions delights, having long bathed in fat rain and sweet dew; fearing that extravagant hearts might sprout and slackness toward eastern tillage arise, [one] therefore reiterated old statutes, issued clear edicts, ordered the responsible offices, promulgated laws and measures, displayed moderation and thrift, and showed the great simplicity.
translations.idiomatic: **entered into** the joys of every quarter after long soaking in imperial bounty; dreading luxury and neglect of spring plowing, he renewed old rules, published clear decrees, charged the bureaus, spread the code, proclaimed austerity, and held up utter simplicity as the model.

sentences.zh: [二]去後宮之麗飾，損乘輿之服御，除工商之淫業，興農桑之上務。

translations.literal: [Two] He removed lavish adornment from the rear palace, cut back chariot and robe supplies, abolished licentious crafts of artisans and merchants, and raised the highest task of farming and sericulture.
translations.idiomatic: [2] He stripped the harem of finery, pared the carriage wardrobe, shut down vain crafts, and put tillage and mulberry first.

sentences.zh: 遂令海內□末而反本，背偽而歸真，女修織紝，男務耕耘，器用陶匏，服尚素玄，恥纖靡而不服，賤奇麗而不珍，捐金於山，沉珠於淵。

translations.literal: Thus he ordered that within the seas □ the secondary and return to the root, turn backs on sham and return to the genuine: women perfected weaving, men labored at plowing; utensils were pottery and gourd; dress honored plain black; they were ashamed of gossamer finery and would not wear it, despised curious splendor and did not treasure it; they cast gold into the hills and sank pearls in the deeps.
translations.idiomatic: So he had the realm abandon the secondary trades for the root, leave pretense for plain truth: women at the loom, men at the hoe; tools of clay and gourd; robes of undyed black; shame at sheer gauze, scorn for gaudy gems — gold thrown back to the hills, pearls dropped in the streams.

sentences.zh: [三]於是百姓滌瑕蕩穢而鏡至清，形神寂漠，耳目不營，嗜欲之原滅，廉正之心生，莫不優遊而自得，玉潤而金聲。

translations.literal: [Three] Then the hundred surnames washed away stain and filth until they mirrored utmost purity; form and spirit grew still and silent, ears and eyes had no false pursuits; the springs of craving dried up, hearts of integrity were born; none failed to wander at ease and find contentment, jade-smooth in bearing and bell-clear in voice.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Common folk scoured away vice until they shone like still water; body and soul fell quiet, senses unhooked from desire; greed died and straight hearts grew; everyone moved at ease, smooth as jade, ringing as metal.

sentences.zh: [四]是以四海之內，學校如林，庠序盈門，獻酬交錯，俎豆莘莘，下舞上歌，蹈德詠仁。

translations.literal: [Four] Therefore within the four seas schools stood thick as woods, village halls thronged at the doors; pledges and toasts crossed back and forth, stands of meat and vessels of grain crowded thick; below they danced, above they sang, treading virtue and hymning benevolence.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Schools clustered like a forest, village schools jammed their doors; cups crossed, ritual vessels piled high; dancers below, singers above, all chanting humanity.

sentences.zh: [五]登降飫宴之禮既畢，因相與嗟歎玄德，讜言弘說，鹹含和而吐氣，頌曰「盛哉乎斯世」！

translations.literal: [Five] When the rites of ascending and descending, surfeit and feast had finished, they together sighed over the dark virtue, spoke great counsels in ringing tones, all held harmony and breathed forth praise, saying, "How flourishing is this age!"
translations.idiomatic: [5] When the banquet protocol ended, they marveled at hidden virtue, traded candid maxims, drew a common breath, and cried, "What an age!"

sentences.zh: [六] 注[一]尚書曰：「平秩東作。

translations.literal: [Six] Note [one] The Documents says: "He ordered the eastern tillage aright.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Commentary [1]: the Documents charge to align eastern spring work."

translations.literal: " The commentary says: "The year begins in spring and then they take up plowing."
translations.idiomatic: " The gloss: the year opens in spring, then comes the plow."

sentences.zh: 注[二]詩大雅曰：「率由舊章。

translations.literal: Note [two] The Greater Ya of the Odes says: "Follow the old statutes.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the Odes bid one follow ancient precedent."

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "Old canons and pattern-texts.
translations.idiomatic: " Zheng Xuan: old statutes and written models."

sentences.zh: 」左傳臧哀伯曰：「大路越席，大羹不致，昭其儉也。

translations.literal: " Zang Aibo in the Zuo Tradition says: "The great chariot with coarse mat, the great stew unseasoned — displayed his frugality.
translations.idiomatic: " Zang Aibo praises plain carriages and unseasoned cauldrons as tokens of thrift."

translations.literal: " Liezi says: "Great simplicity is the beginning of substance."
translations.idiomatic: " Liezi calls great simplicity the root of being."

sentences.zh: 注[三]前書文帝詔曰：「農，天下之本也，而人或不務本而事末。

translations.literal: Note [three] The Former Documents, Emperor Wen edict says: "Farming is the root of the realm, yet some men do not attend to the root but pursue the branch.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Wen of Han warned that trade lures men off the land."

translations.literal: " The Yin-yi says: "Root means farming.
translations.idiomatic: " Gloss: root is agriculture."

translations.literal: Branch means merchant.
translations.idiomatic: Branch means trade.

translations.literal: " Turning backs on sham means discarding carved ornament.
translations.idiomatic: " Leaving sham is shedding carved excess."

translations.literal: Returning to the genuine honors plain substance.
translations.idiomatic: Returning to truth means prizing the unadorned.

sentences.zh: 杜預注左傳曰：「織紝，織繒布也。

translations.literal: Du Yu commentary on the Zuo Tradition says: "Zhi ren means weaving silk cloth.
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu on the Zuo zhuan: zhi ren is weaving bolt silk."

translations.literal: " The Record of Rites says: "Utensils use pottery and gourd.
translations.idiomatic: " The Li ji limits vessels to clay and gourd."

translations.literal: " Tao means earthenware.
translations.idiomatic: " Tao means fired clay ware."

translations.literal: Pao means bottle gourd.
translations.idiomatic: Pao is the hollow gourd.

sentences.zh: 陸賈新語曰：「聖人不用珠玉而寶其身，故舜□黃金於□巖之山，捐珠玉於五湖之川，以杜淫邪之欲也。」

translations.literal: Lu Jia New Discourses says: "The sage does not use pearls and jade but treasures his own person; therefore Shun cast gold into the mountain of the dark cliff and cast pearls and jade into the rivers of the five lakes, to block the desires of licentious evil."
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia writes that the sage values his person over gems, so Shun threw gold into a craggy mountain and pearls into the five lakes, choking off decadent craving.

sentences.zh: 注[四]瑕穢猶過惡也，楊雄集曰：「滌瑕蕩穢。

translations.literal: Note [four]: Xia hui means faults and evils; Yang Xiong collection says: "Wash away stain and scour filth.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: blemish means moral stain; Yang Xiong speaks of washing it away."

sentences.zh: 」淮南子曰：「形者生之捨，神者生之制也。

translations.literal: " The Huainanzi says: "The body is the house of life; the spirit is the master of life.
translations.idiomatic: " The Huainanzi says the body shelters life while spirit rules it."

translations.literal: " It also says: "Harmony and compliance in still silence.
translations.idiomatic: " It praises harmony in quiet solitude."

translations.literal: " The Documents says: "Do not enslave ear and eye; the hundred affairs will be true.
translations.idiomatic: " The Shang shu warns against enslaving the senses so that all business stays upright."

sentences.zh: 」淮南子曰：「吾所謂有天下者，自得而已。

translations.literal: " The Huainanzi says: "What I call possessing the realm is simply finding contentment in oneself.
translations.idiomatic: " The Huainanzi defines ruling the world as resting content in oneself."

translations.literal: " The Record of Rites records Confucius as saying;
translations.idiomatic: " The Li ji quotes Confucius:

sentences.zh: 「君子比德於玉焉，溫潤而澤，仁也。

translations.literal: "The gentleman compares his virtue to jade — warm, smooth, and lustrous: that is benevolence.
translations.idiomatic: "The gentleman likens his character to jade, warm and glossy: that is humanity."

translations.literal: " Mencius says of Confucius that his virtue was like the sound of metal."
translations.idiomatic: " Mencius says Confucius had the ring of metal in his virtue."

translations.literal: Note [five] The Former Documents: when Emperor Ping established *
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: the Han shu records Ping as founding

translations.literal: (ju)
translations.idiomatic: (state)

translations.literal: *school officers.
translations.idiomatic: schools for the realm.

sentences.zh: 郡國曰學，縣道邑及侯國曰校，鄉曰庠，聚曰序。

translations.literal: In commanderies and kingdoms they were called xue; in counties, circuits, towns, and marquisates xiao; in townships xiang; in hamlets xu.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies had xue, counties xiao, townships xiang, hamlets xu.

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Pledges and toasts crossed back and forth.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes picture cups crossing in pledge."

translations.literal: " Shen shen means very many.
translations.idiomatic: " Shen shen means crowded plenty."

translations.literal: Read suo-jin fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie suo-jin.

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says;
translations.idiomatic: The Li ji adds:

translations.literal: "The singers were above — honoring the voices of the noble.
translations.idiomatic: "Singers stood aloft to honor noble voices."

sentences.zh: 」又「嗟歎之不足，故手之舞之，足之蹈之」。

translations.literal: " And "when sighing was not enough, they waved their hands and stamped their feet."
translations.idiomatic: " When sighs failed, they danced."

translations.literal: Note [six] The Odes says: "The drinking reached satiety.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: the Odes speak of the banquet surfeit."

sentences.zh: 」毛萇注云：「不脫屨升堂謂之飫。

translations.literal: " Mao Chang commentary says: "To ascend the hall without removing shoes is called yu.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao Chang defines yu as a private meal where shoes stay on."

translations.literal: " Yu means private.
translations.idiomatic: " Yu means an informal feast."

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The dark virtue ascended to notice.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu says hidden virtue rose until it was heard."

translations.literal: The Forest of Characters says: "Dang means fine words.
translations.idiomatic: The Zi lin defines dang as candid counsel."

translations.literal: Read dang."
translations.idiomatic: Read like dang."

sentences.zh: 今論者但知誦虞夏之書，詠殷周之詩，講羲文之易，論孔氏之春秋，罕能精古今之清濁，究漢德之所由。

translations.literal: Today the disputants only know to chant the books of Yu and Xia, sing the odes of Yin and Zhou, expound the Changes of Xi and Wen, discuss the Spring and Autumn of Confucius — rarely can they sift the clear from the turbid of past and present, or trace whence Han virtue arose.
translations.idiomatic: Modern scholars mouth the ancient classics yet seldom weigh good against bad across the ages or ask where Han power truly came from.

sentences.zh: [一]唯子頗識舊典，又徒馳騁乎末流。

translations.literal: [One] Only you, sir, know something of the old canons, yet you still gallop in the lower currents.
translations.idiomatic: [1] You alone recall the old texts, yet you race along shallow channels.

sentences.zh: 溫故知新已難，而知德者鮮矣！

translations.literal: To warm the old and know the new is already hard; those who know true virtue are few!
translations.idiomatic: Reviewing the past to grasp the new is hard enough; men who know real virtue are rare.

sentences.zh: [二]且夫辟界西戎，險阻四塞，修其防禦，孰與處乎土中，平夷洞達，萬方輻湊？

translations.literal: [Two] Moreover, to ward off the western Rong behind barriers, with perilous passes closing four sides, and repair those defenses — how does that compare with dwelling in the center of the land, level and open, the myriad regions converging like spokes to a hub?
translations.idiomatic: [2] What is guarding western passes to a cramped capital beside sitting in the plain heartland where all roads meet like spokes?

sentences.zh: [三]秦領九嵕，涇渭之川，曷若四瀆五嶽，帶河泝洛，圖書之淵？

translations.literal: [Three] The Qinling range and Mount Jiuwu, the streams of Jing and Wei — how do they match the four great rivers and five marchmounts, the Yellow River girdling, the Luo traced upward, the deep well of the charts and documents?
translations.idiomatic: [3] How can Qin ridges and Jing-Wei rival the four rivers, five peaks, the book-bearing Yellow River and Luo?

sentences.zh: [四]建章甘泉，館御列仙，孰與靈台明堂，統和天人？

translations.literal: [Four] The palaces of Jianzhang and Sweet Dew, halls serving ranks of immortals — how do they compare with the Spirit Terrace and Bright Hall, harmonizing Heaven and man?
translations.idiomatic: [4] Qin-style immortal towers beside the Han Spirit Terrace and Bright Hall that knit Heaven to humanity?

sentences.zh: [五]太液昆明，鳥獸之囿，曷若辟雍海流，道德之富？

translations.literal: [Five] Taiye and Kunming, parks for birds and beasts — how do they match the Biyong with seas of water around it, the wealth of the Way and its virtue?
translations.idiomatic: [5] Artificial lakes and game parks beside the Ring Hall encircled by water, treasury of moral teaching?

sentences.zh: [六]遊俠踰侈，犯義侵禮，孰與同履法度，翼翼濟濟也？

translations.literal: [Six] Knights-errant who break bounds in extravagance, violate duty and encroach on ritual — how does that compare with treading together the laws and measures, solemn and stately?
translations.idiomatic: [6] Lawless bravoes against walking in measured law?

sentences.zh: [七]子徒習秦阿房之造天，而不知京洛之有制也；

translations.literal: [Seven] You only study how the Epang palace of Qin scraped the sky, yet you do not know that the eastern capitals had their proper system;
translations.idiomatic: [7] You memorize Epang touching heaven but ignore the regulated splendor of Luoyang;

sentences.zh: 識函谷之可關，而不知王者之無外也。

translations.literal: you know Hangu Pass can be barred, yet you do not know that the true king has nothing outside his realm."
translations.idiomatic: you know Hangu can be locked yet forget that the royal domain knows no frontier."

sentences.zh: 」[八] 注[一]伏羲畫八卦，文王作卦辭，孔子作春秋。

translations.literal: " [Eight] Note [one] Fuxi drew the eight trigrams, King Wen wrote the hexagram statements, Confucius composed the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: " [8] Commentary [1]: Fuxi drew the trigrams, Wen wrote the judgments, Confucius edited the Chunqiu.

translations.literal: Clear and turbid means good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: Clear versus turbid parallels good versus evil.

translations.literal: Note [two] Mo liu means the lower current.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: downstream means lesser schools.

translations.literal: It refers to the various masters.
translations.idiomatic: That is the philosophers.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「不入於道德，放縱於末流。

translations.literal: The Former Documents says: "They do not enter the path of virtue but drift in the lower currents.
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu warns against drifting from the Way into shallow currents."

sentences.zh: 」論語孔子曰：「溫故知新，可以為師矣。

translations.literal: " The Analects records Confucius as saying: "Review the old and know the new — then you may be a teacher.
translations.idiomatic: " Confucius says reviewing the old yields the new, fit to teach."

translations.literal: " It also says: "You — those who know virtue are few."
translations.idiomatic: " He tells Zhong You that few know virtue."

translations.literal: Note [three] Pi means distant; read pi-yi fan.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: pi means remote; fanqie pi-yi.

sentences.zh: 戰國策蘇秦說孟嘗君曰：「秦，四塞之國也。

translations.literal: The Intrigues of the Warring States: Su Qin said to Lord Mengchang: "Qin is a state closed on four sides.
translations.idiomatic: Su Qin calls Qin a land boxed by four barriers."

sentences.zh: 」高誘注云：「四面有山關之固，故曰四塞之國。

translations.literal: " Gao You commentary says: "On four sides mountains and passes make it firm; hence it is called a four-sealed state.
translations.idiomatic: " Gao You explains the ring of mountains."

translations.literal: " Fang yu means passes and prohibitions.
translations.idiomatic: " Fang yu means frontier bans."

translations.literal: Fu cou means like spokes gathering at the hub.
translations.idiomatic: Fu cou images every road meeting at the axle hub.

sentences.zh: 前書武帝詔吾丘壽王曰「子在朕前之時，知略輻湊」也。

translations.literal: The Former Documents, Emperor Wu edict to Wuqiu Shouwang says: "When you were before Us, stratagems gathered like spokes."
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu quotes Wudi praising Shouwang for clustering counsel like spokes.

translations.literal: Note [four] The four du are the Yangzi, Yellow River, Huai, and Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: the four channels are Jiang, He, Huai, and Ji.

sentences.zh: 河圖曰：「天有四表，以布精魄，地有四瀆，以出圖書。

translations.literal: The River Chart says: "Heaven has four outer markers to spread soul and essence; earth has four channels to bring forth charts and documents.
translations.idiomatic: The Hetu pairs heaven four tables with earth four rivers bearing the books."

sentences.zh: 」爾雅曰：「太山為東嶽，衡山為南嶽，華山為西嶽，恆山為北嶽，嵩山為中岳。

translations.literal: " The Erya says: "Mount Tai is the eastern marchmount, Heng the southern, Hua the western, Heng the northern, Song the central peak.
translations.idiomatic: " The Erya lists the five sacred peaks."

sentences.zh: 」圖書之泉謂河洛也，易系辭曰「河出圖，洛出書」也。

translations.literal: " The spring of charts and documents means the Yellow River and Luo; the Changes "Appended Remarks" says "the River brought forth the chart, the Luo brought forth the writing."
translations.idiomatic: " Chart and writing mean the Hetu and Luoshu, per the Zhou yi."

sentences.zh: 注[五]館御謂設台以進御神仙也。

translations.literal: Note [five] Guan yu means setting up terraces to welcome immortal guests.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: guan yu are stages built for immortal séances.

sentences.zh: 禮含文嘉曰「禮，天子靈台，以考觀天人之際，法陰陽之會」也。

translations.literal: The Li han wen jia says: "By ritual the Son of Heaven has a Spirit Terrace to examine the juncture of Heaven and man and model the meeting of yin and yang."
translations.idiomatic: The Li han wen jia says the sovereign terrace reads heaven-human signs.

sentences.zh: 注[六]三輔黃圖曰「辟雍，水四周於外，像四海」也。

translations.literal: Note [six] The Yellow Map of the Three Metropolitan Areas says: "The Biyong has water on four sides without, imaging the four seas."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: the San fu huang tu says the Ring Hall is moated like the four seas.

sentences.zh: 注[七]遊俠，即西賓云「鄉曲豪俊，遊俠之雄」。

translations.literal: Note [seven] Knights-errant are what the western guest called "local stalwarts, champions among wandering knights."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: you xia matches the western visitor bravoes.

sentences.zh: 踰侈謂「列肆侈於姬﹑姜」等也。

translations.literal: Yu chi refers to "rows of shops more splendid than Ji and Jiang" and the like.
translations.idiomatic: Yu chi cites the boast of markets finer than famous beauties.

translations.literal: The Erya says: "Yi yi means reverent.
translations.idiomatic: The Erya glosses yi yi as solemn awe."

translations.literal: " The Odes says: "So many officers, stately.
translations.idiomatic: " The Odes praise a host of dignified ministers."

translations.literal: " Mao Chang commentary says: "Ji ji means abundant dignity in bearing."
translations.idiomatic: " Mao Chang reads ji ji as imposing deportment."

sentences.zh: 注[八]史記曰，秦始皇作阿房宮。

translations.literal: Note [eight] The Records say that the First Emperor of Qin built Epang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [8]: the Shi ji credits Qin Shihuang with Epang.

translations.literal: Zao means to reach.
translations.idiomatic: Zao here means to touch or reach.

translations.literal: The Gongyang Commentary says: "The true king has nothing outside."
translations.idiomatic: The Gong yang zhuan says the royal realm is boundless.

sentences.zh: 主人之辭未終，西都賓矍然失容，逡巡降階，惵然意下，捧手欲辭。

translations.literal: Before the host words had ended, the guest from the western capital started in alarm, lost color, drew back and descended the steps, downcast in spirit, clasping his hands as if to take leave.
translations.idiomatic: Mid-speech the western guest blanched, shrank down the stairs, and made to withdraw.

sentences.zh: 主人曰：「復位，今將喻子五篇之詩。

translations.literal: The host said: "Return to your seat; now I shall expound to you the poem in five sections.
translations.idiomatic: The host bids him sit again and hear five cantos.

sentences.zh: 」[一]賓既卒業，乃稱曰：「美哉乎此詩！

translations.literal: " [One] When the guest had finished hearing them, he declared: "How beautiful is this poem!
translations.idiomatic: " [1] When the lesson ended, he cried, "What a poem!"

sentences.zh: 義正乎楊雄，事實乎相如，非唯主人之好學，蓋乃遭遇乎斯時也。

translations.literal: Its righteousness is as straight as that of Yang Xiong, its facts as solid as those of Sima Xiangru — not only because the host loves learning, but because he has met this very age.
translations.idiomatic: Its moral line rivals Yang Xiong, its matter Sima Xiangru — credit the age as much as the scholar.

sentences.zh: [二]小子狂簡，不知所裁，既聞正道，請終身誦之。

translations.literal: [Two] I am a rash young fellow who does not know how to trim my course; having heard the correct Way, I beg to chant it all my life.
translations.idiomatic: [2] I am brash and unformed, yet now that I have heard the true teaching I would recite it forever.

sentences.zh: 」其詩曰：[三] 注[一]說文曰：「矍，視遽之貌。

translations.literal: " The poem says: [Three] Note [one] The Shuowen says: "Jue is the look of hasty seeing.
translations.idiomatic: " The poem begins. Commentary [1]: the Shuowen defines jue as a startled glance."

translations.literal: " Read xu-fu fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Fanqie xu-fu."

translations.literal: The Zhou Documents says: "Draw near with awe and be fearful.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou shu speaks of approaching in dread."

translations.literal: " Die means something like fear; read tu-jia fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Die means trembling; fanqie tu-jia."

translations.literal: Yu means to tell.
translations.idiomatic: Yu means to explain.

sentences.zh: 注[二]楊雄作長楊﹑羽獵賦，司馬相如作子虛﹑上林賦，並文雖藻麗，其事□誕，不如主人之言義正事實也。

translations.literal: Note [two] Yang Xiong wrote Changyang and Plume Hunt rhapsodies; Sima Xiangru wrote Sir Vacuous and Upper Forest — though their lines are ornate, their events are vain and extravagant, not matching the host words where righteousness is straight and facts are solid.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Yang and Sima dazzle but invent; the host stays true.

sentences.zh: 注[三]論語孔子曰：「吾黨之小子狂簡，斐然成章，不知所以裁之。

translations.literal: Note [three] The Analects records Confucius as saying: "The lads in my village are rash and rough; they have cut a pattern yet do not know how to trim it.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Confucius calls his youths talented but untutored."

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「不忮不求，何用不臧，子路終身誦之。」

translations.literal: " It also says: "Without envy, without greed — what use is there in not being good?" Zilu chanted it all his life."
translations.idiomatic: " He also praises freedom from envy; Zilu made that his lifelong refrain."

sentences.zh: l明堂詩：於昭明堂，明堂孔陽；

translations.literal: Bright Hall ode: Oh, the bright hall, the hall so radiant;
translations.idiomatic: The Ming tang song: "Bright, the Bright Hall, blazing bright;

translations.literal: the sage emperor performs the lineage sacrifice, solemn and splendid.
translations.idiomatic: the sage sovereign sacrifices, grave and glorious."

translations.literal: [One] High God feasts; the five positions proceed in order;
translations.idiomatic: [1] High God dines; the five thrones stand in sequence;

translations.literal: who matches him? Shizu Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: who shares his place? Emperor Guangwu, the Founding Ancestor.

translations.literal: [Two] All within heaven, all upon the earth — each performs its duty;
translations.idiomatic: [2] Under heaven, on earth, every office does its part;

translations.literal: oh, the lasting radiance — truly harboring many blessings.
translations.idiomatic: ah, the steady glow — truly holding many blessings.

translations.literal: [Three]
translations.idiomatic: The third stanza of the hymn ends here.

sentences.zh: 注[一]於音烏，歎美之辭也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Wu is read wu — an exclamation of praise.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: wu is a sigh of admiration.

translations.literal: The Zhou hymn of the Odes says: "Oh, brightness in heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou song cries, "Bright in heaven!"

translations.literal: " Kong means very.
translations.idiomatic: " Kong means exceedingly."

translations.literal: Yang means bright.
translations.idiomatic: Yang means shining.

translations.literal: A state air says: "Our vermilion is very yang.
translations.idiomatic: A Guo feng line says our crimson is vivid."

sentences.zh: 」聖皇宗祀謂祭光武於明堂也。

translations.literal: " Sheng huang zong si means sacrificing to Guangwu in the Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: " Sheng huang zong si is the Ming tang offering to Guangwu."

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「穆穆煌煌，宜君宜王。

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Solemn, splendid — fit to be lord, fit to be king.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say solemn splendor fits a king."

translations.literal: " Mu mu means reverent.
translations.idiomatic: " Mu mu is grave respect."

translations.literal: Huang huang means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: Huang huang is radiant beauty.

sentences.zh: 注[二]前書曰：「天神貴者太一，太一佐曰五帝。

translations.literal: Note [two] The Former Documents says: "Among celestial spirits the highest is Taiyi; the assistants of Taiyi are called the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the Han shu ranks Taiyi above the Five Thearchs."

translations.literal: " The five positions are the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: " The five seats are the five colored emperors."

sentences.zh: 河圖曰：「蒼帝靈威仰，赤帝赤熛怒，黃帝含樞紐，白帝白招矩，黑帝□光紀。

translations.literal: The River Chart names the five thearchs: the Green Thearch Lingweiyang, the Red Thearch Chibiaonu, the Yellow Thearch Hanshuniu, the White Thearch Baizhaoju, the Black Thearch Yeguangji.
translations.idiomatic: The Hetu registers the five directional sovereigns by their cult epithets.

sentences.zh: 」楊雄河東賦曰：「靈只既饗，五位時□。

translations.literal: " Yang Xiong Hedong Rhapsody says: "When the spirits had feasted, the five positions aligned in their season.
translations.idiomatic: " Yang Xiong Hedong fu: after the gods feasted, the five thrones took their seasonal stations."

translations.literal: " It means each is sacrificed to according to its direction.
translations.idiomatic: " Each deity received offering from its proper quarter."

sentences.zh: 注[三]詩小雅曰：「溥天之下，莫非王土。

translations.literal: Note [three] The Lesser Ya says: "Under wide heaven, none is not the king soil.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: the Shi jing says no inch of earth is not the king under heaven."

translations.literal: Of all who are guests upon the soil, none is not the king minister.
translations.idiomatic: Every soul on that soil owes fealty to the throne."

translations.literal: " Pu also means universal.
translations.idiomatic: " Pu means all-embracing."

sentences.zh: 孝經曰：「四海之內，各以其職來助祭。

translations.literal: The Classic of Filial Piety says: "Within the four seas each came with his office to assist at the sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiao jing says the four seas sent officers to help at the rites."

translations.literal: " The Shang hymn says: "Oh, how grand! Ah, how fine!
translations.idiomatic: " The Shang song cries, "How vast, how splendid!"

translations.literal: " Yi means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: " Yi is a sigh of praise."

translations.literal: Yun means truly.
translations.idiomatic: Yun means indeed or truly.

translations.literal: Huai means to come.
translations.idiomatic: Huai here means to draw near.

translations.literal: The Greater Ya says: "Truly we embrace many blessings."
translations.idiomatic: The Greater Ya prays to hold many blessings.

sentences.zh: l辟雍詩：乃流辟雍，辟雍湯湯；

translations.literal: Biyong ode: Thus flows the Biyong, the Biyong surging wide;
translations.idiomatic: The Ring Hall hymn: "The moat runs round the Biyong, rolling wide;

translations.literal: the sage emperor halts his horses and builds boats into a bridge.
translations.idiomatic: the sage sovereign reins in, spans the stream with linked boats."

translations.literal: [One] Hoary state elders — fathers and elder brothers alike;
translations.idiomatic: [1] White-haired elders of the state — each a father, each an elder brother;

translations.literal: grave in every motion, filial and fraternal, bright in virtue.
translations.idiomatic: grave in bearing, shining with filial piety and brotherly love.

translations.literal: [Two] Oh, august Grand Above — you show us the Han way;
translations.idiomatic: [2] O radiant Grand Exemplar — you reveal the Han path;

translations.literal: the vast transformation is numinous; long may we gaze on its completion.
translations.idiomatic: the great transformation works like spirit; long may we behold its consummation.

translations.literal: [Three]
translations.idiomatic: The third stanza of the Biyong hymn ends here.

translations.literal: Note [one] Tang tang describes flowing water.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: tang tang is the rush of water.

translations.literal: Ma means to approach.
translations.idiomatic: Ma means to draw rein or halt.

translations.literal: The Lesser Ya says: "Fang Shu halts his horses.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Ya has Fang Shu rein in."

translations.literal: " The Greater Ya says: "He built boats into a bridge.
translations.idiomatic: " The Greater Ya tells of bridging with boats."

translations.literal: " Mao Chang commentary says: "The Son of Heaven builds boats.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao Chang notes the royal boat-bridge."

translations.literal: " Zao means to reach; it means linking boats to make a floating bridge.
translations.idiomatic: " Zao means to join; here, chaining boats as a pontoon."

sentences.zh: 注[二]說文曰：「皤皤，老人貌也。

translations.literal: Note [two] The Shuowen says: "Bo bo is the appearance of old men.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the Shuowen defines bo bo as white-haired age."

translations.literal: " Read bo-he fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Fanqie bo-he."

sentences.zh: 孝經援神契曰：「天子尊事三老，兄事五更。

translations.literal: The Classic of Filial Piety, Assorted Proofs of Spirit says: "The Son of Heaven honors the three elders as fathers and treats the five renewals as elder brothers.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiao jing yuan shen qi prescribes honoring three elders and five geng."

translations.literal: " Yi yi means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: " Yi yi means stately grace."

translations.literal: The Odes says: "His bearing was yi yi.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes praise yi yi deportment."

sentences.zh: 」爾雅曰：「善父母為孝，善兄弟為友。」

translations.literal: " The Erya says: "To treat parents well is xiao; to treat brothers well is you."
translations.idiomatic: " The Erya defines filial piety and fraternal duty."

translations.literal: Note [three] Yu he is a sigh of praise.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: yu he is exclamatory praise.

sentences.zh: 太上謂太古立德賢聖之人。

translations.literal: Tai shang means the men of high antiquity who established virtue — worthies and sages.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Above names the sages who first set virtue in high antiquity.

sentences.zh: 並著養老之禮，今我漢家遵行之也。

translations.literal: They all set forth the rites for nurturing the aged; now our Han house follows them.
translations.idiomatic: They framed the old-age rites; Han now walks in their footsteps.

translations.literal: Hong means great.
translations.idiomatic: Hong means vast.

sentences.zh: 文子曰：「執玄德於心，化馳如神。

translations.literal: Wenzi says: "Grasp dark virtue in the heart — transformation speeds like a spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Wenzi says hold dark virtue inward and change races like a god."

sentences.zh: 」詩周頌曰：「我客戾止，永觀厥成。

translations.literal: " The Zhou hymn says: "My guests have arrived; long may we gaze on the completion.
translations.idiomatic: " The Zhou song welcomes guests to witness the consummation."

translations.literal: " The Erya says: "Guan means to display."
translations.idiomatic: " The Erya glosses guan as to show."

sentences.zh: l靈台詩：乃經靈台，靈台既崇；

translations.literal: Spirit Terrace ode: Thus we laid out the Spirit Terrace; the Spirit Terrace rose high;
translations.idiomatic: The Ling tai song: "We traced the Spirit Terrace; the terrace climbed high;

translations.literal: the emperor labored to ascend in season, then examined auspicious signs.
translations.idiomatic: the sovereign climbed at the proper hour to read the good omens."

translations.literal: [Two] The three luminaries spread their essence; the five phases array in order;
translations.idiomatic: [2] Sun, moon, and stars pour forth radiance; the five agents fall in sequence;

translations.literal: gentle, gentle — the auspicious wind; slow, slow — the sweet rain.
translations.idiomatic: soft blows the lucky breeze; gentle falls the sweet rain.

translations.literal: [Two] The hundred grains grow thick; the myriad plants flourish;
translations.idiomatic: [2] Grain crops teem; every herb runs riot;

translations.literal: again and again we pray for rich years — oh, how we rejoice together!
translations.idiomatic: year after year we ask abundance — O how we share the joy!

translations.literal: [Three]
translations.idiomatic: The third stanza of the Spirit Terrace hymn ends here.

sentences.zh: 注[一]詩大雅曰：「經始靈台。

translations.literal: Note [one] The Greater Ya says: "He began to trace the Spirit Terrace.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: the Greater Ya opens with laying out the terrace."

translations.literal: " Chong means high.
translations.idiomatic: " Chong means lofty."

translations.literal: Shi deng means ascending it according to the season.
translations.idiomatic: Climbing at the proper season.

translations.literal: Xiu means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: Xiu means favorable or fine.

translations.literal: Zheng means verification.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng means proof or omen.

translations.literal: Note [two] The three luminaries are sun, moon, and stars.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the three lights are sun, moon, and stars.

translations.literal: Xuan means to spread.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan means to broadcast.

translations.literal: Jing means bright.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means clear brilliance.

translations.literal: The five phases are water, fire, metal, wood, and earth.
translations.idiomatic: The five agents are water, fire, metal, wood, earth.

sentences.zh: 布序謂各順其性，無謬沴也。

translations.literal: Bu xu means each follows its nature without perverse harm.
translations.idiomatic: Arrayed order means each element keeps its nature without blight.

translations.literal: Xi xi means harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: Xi xi describes a mild, blending wind.

translations.literal: The Lesser Ya says: "Soft blows the valley wind.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Ya line is soft valley wind."

sentences.zh: 」禮斗威儀曰：「君政頌平，則祥風至。

translations.literal: " The Li dou wei yi says: "When the ruler government is balanced, the auspicious wind arrives.
translations.idiomatic: " The Li dou wei yi ties mild rule to lucky winds."

translations.literal: " Song Jun commentary says: "That is the bright wind.
translations.idiomatic: " Song Jun identifies it with the jing wind."

translations.literal: " Qi qi means leisurely.
translations.idiomatic: " Qi qi means slow and even."

translations.literal: The Lesser Ya says: "The rising rain is qi qi.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Ya says the rain comes qi qi, gently."

sentences.zh: 」尚書考靈耀曰「熒惑順行，甘雨時」也。

translations.literal: " The Documents, Examining the Spirits says: "When Mars marches straight, sweet rain comes in season."
translations.idiomatic: " The Shang shu kao ling yao ties orderly Mars to timely rain."

sentences.zh: 注[三]百，言非一也，尚書洪範曰：「百谷用成。

translations.literal: Note [three] "Hundred" means not only one; the Documents, Great Plan says: "The hundred grains thereby ripen.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: "hundred" means many; the Hong fan promises ripe grain."

translations.literal: " Qin qin describes luxuriant growth.
translations.idiomatic: " Qin qin pictures thick standing crops."

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The many grasses flourish thick.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu says the grasses grow lush."

translations.literal: " The Erya says: "Fan wu means abundant.
translations.idiomatic: " The Erya glosses fan wu as rich growth."

translations.literal: " The Zhou hymn says: "We soothed the myriad regions, again and again had rich years.
translations.idiomatic: " The Zhou song prays for many fat years."

translations.literal: " It also says: "Oh, how great was Zhou in that time.
translations.idiomatic: " Another line acclaims Zhou at its height."

translations.literal: " Yu is read wu.
translations.idiomatic: " Yu is read wu."

sentences.zh: 詩小雅曰：「君子樂胥，受天之祜。

translations.literal: The Lesser Ya says: "The gentleman delights with his fellows and receives Heaven blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Ya says the lord rejoices with companions and wins heaven blessing."

translations.literal: " The commentary says: "Xu is a name for those who have talent and wisdom."
translations.idiomatic: " The gloss calls xu the wise companion."

sentences.zh: l寶鼎詩：岳修貢兮川效珍，吐金景兮歊浮雲。

translations.literal: Treasure Tripod ode: Peaks send tribute, rivers yield treasure; they spit golden light and breathe clouds afloat.
translations.idiomatic: The precious cauldron song: mountains send tribute, rivers offer gems; golden gleam rises, mist floats on the wind.

sentences.zh: 寶鼎見兮色紛縕，煥其炳兮被龍文。

translations.literal: The treasure tripod appears — colors interwoven; blazing bright, it wears dragon pattern.
translations.idiomatic: The tripod shows itself in woven colors, blazing with dragon designs.

sentences.zh: [一]登祖廟兮享聖神，昭靈德兮彌億年。

translations.literal: [One] Ascending the ancestral temple, feasting the holy spirits — manifesting numinous virtue for a billion years.
translations.idiomatic: [1] It enters the lineage shrine to feast the gods, showing sacred power for endless ages.

sentences.zh: [二] 注[一]謂永平六年王雒山得寶鼎，廬江太守獻之。

translations.literal: [Two] Note [one] It refers to Yongping 6 when a treasure tripod was found on Mount Wangluo and the administrator of Lujiang presented it.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Commentary [1]: Yongping 6, tripod from Wangluo Mountain, sent in by the Lujiang governor.

translations.literal: Jing means light.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means radiance.

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: "Xiao is the appearance of vapor rising.
translations.idiomatic: The Shuowen defines xiao as steaming breath."

translations.literal: " Read huo-jiao fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Fanqie huo-jiao."

sentences.zh: 史記曰：「秦武王與孟悅舉龍文之鼎。」

translations.literal: The Records says: "King Wu of Qin and Meng Yue lifted the dragon-pattern tripod."
translations.idiomatic: The Shi ji tells of King Wu of Qin hoisting the dragon tripod.

sentences.zh: 注[二]時明帝詔曰：「其以礿祭之日，陳鼎於廟，以備器用。

translations.literal: Note [two] At that time Emperor Ming edict said: "On the day of the yue sacrifice display the tripod in the temple to complete the ritual vessels.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Mingdi ordered the tripod shown at the yue offering.

translations.literal: " Mi means to the end.
translations.idiomatic: " Mi means lasting to the end."

translations.literal: Ten thousand ten thousands is called yi.
translations.idiomatic: A myriad of myriads is called yi.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「公其以予萬億年敬天之休。」

translations.literal: The Documents says: "You shall respect heaven blessing for myriads of yi years."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu prays for trillions of years honoring heaven grace.

sentences.zh: l白雉詩：啟靈篇兮披瑞圖，獲白雉兮效素烏。

translations.literal: White Pheasant ode: Opening the spirit documents, spreading the auspicious charts — we caught the white pheasant, presented the white crow.
translations.idiomatic: The white pheasant song: unrolling river charts, we took the white pheasant and offered the white crow.

sentences.zh: [一]髮皓羽兮奮翹英，容絜朗兮於淳精。

translations.literal: [One] Unfurling white plumes, raising splendid tail — the form is pure and bright, embodying perfect essence.
translations.idiomatic: [1] White feathers spread, tail lifted high — a form clean and bright, distilled spirit.

sentences.zh: [二]章皇德兮侔周成，永延長兮膺天慶。

translations.literal: [Two] Displaying royal virtue, matching King Cheng — may it lengthen forever and receive heaven celebration.
translations.idiomatic: [2] It proclaims royal virtue like King Cheng; may it lengthen and win heaven praise.

sentences.zh: [三] 注[一]靈篇謂河洛之書也。

translations.literal: [Three] Note [one] Ling pian means the writings of the River and Luo.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Commentary [1]: spirit documents are Hetu and Luoshu.

sentences.zh: 固集此題篇云「白雉素烏歌」，故兼言「效素烏」。

translations.literal: Gu titled this piece "Song of the White Pheasant and White Crow," hence it also says "presented the white crow."
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu headlined the poem for both birds, so the text mentions the crow.

translations.literal: Note [two] Hao means white.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: hao means white.

translations.literal: Qiao means tail.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao means the tail feathers.

sentences.zh: 春秋元命包曰：「烏者陽之精。」

translations.literal: The Chunqiu yuan ming bao says: "The crow is the essence of yang."
translations.idiomatic: The Yuan ming bao calls the crow yang essence.

translations.literal: Note [three] Zhang means to display.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: zhang means to make manifest.

translations.literal: Mou means equal.
translations.idiomatic: Mou means to match or equal.

sentences.zh: 孝經援神契曰：「周成王時，越裳獻白雉。

translations.literal: The Classic of Filial Piety, Assorted Proofs of Spirit says: "In the time of King Cheng of Zhou, Yuechang presented a white pheasant.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuan shen qi recalls Yuechang tribute to King Cheng."

translations.literal: Qing is read like qing (minister).
translations.idiomatic: Qing here rhymes like the word for minister.

sentences.zh: 及肅宗雅好文章，固愈得幸，數入讀書禁中，或連日繼夜。

translations.literal: By the time Emperor Zhang loved belles-lettres, Ban Gu grew still more favored, repeatedly entering the forbidden quarters to read books, sometimes for days and nights in succession.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhangdi cultivated literature, Ban Gu won deeper favor, often reading in the inner palace day and night.

sentences.zh: 每行巡狩，輒獻上賦頌，朝廷有大議，使難問公卿，辯論於前，賞賜恩寵甚渥。

translations.literal: Whenever the court went on tour he presented fu and eulogies; on great deliberations he was sent to challenge the dukes and ministers, debating before the throne — rewards and kindness were exceedingly rich.
translations.idiomatic: On each imperial tour he offered rhapsodies; in great councils he cross-examined ministers before the throne and earned lavish gifts.

sentences.zh: 固自以二世才術，位不過郎，[一]感東方朔﹑楊雄自論，以不遭蘇﹑張﹑范﹑蔡之時，作賓戲以自通焉。

translations.literal: Ban Gu himself, because for two generations the family talent had not risen past Gentleman rank, [one] moved by Dongfang Shuo and Yang Xiong writing self-rebukes, since he had not encountered an age of Su, Zhang, Fan, and Cai, composed the Play of the Guest to express his mind.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu, seeing two generations stall at Gentleman rank, took heart from Dongfang Shuo and Yang Xiong; born outside the age when Su Qin, Zhang Yi, Fan Ju, and Cai Ze could open careers, he wrote the Ke xi to speak his mind.

translations.literal: [Two] Later he was promoted to marshal of the Black Tortoise Gate.
translations.idiomatic: [2] He was later moved to Black Tortoise Gate marshal.

sentences.zh: [三]天子會諸儒講論五經，作白虎通德論，令固撰集其事。

translations.literal: [Three] When the Son of Heaven assembled the scholars to debate the Five Classics and compose the Comprehensive Meaning of the White Tiger Hall, he ordered Ban Gu to compile the record.
translations.idiomatic: [3] At the White Tiger symposium on the Five Classics, the emperor told Ban Gu to edit the proceedings.

translations.literal: [Four] Note [one]: Two generations means Ban Biao and Ban Gu.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Commentary [1]: two generations are father Biao and son Gu.

sentences.zh: 注[二]東方朔荅客難曰：「使蘇秦﹑張儀與僕並生，曾不得掌故，安敢望侍郎乎？

translations.literal: Note [two]: Dongfang Shuo Answer to a Guest says: "Had Su Qin and Zhang Yi been born with your servant, I could not even have become a clerk — how would I dare hope for Gentleman rank?
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Dongfang Shuo laments missing great rhetors of the Warring States.

sentences.zh: 」楊雄解嘲曰：「范睢，魏之亡命也。

translations.literal: " Yang Xiong, Dispelling Ridicule, says: "Fan Ju was a fugitive from Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong in the Jie zhao recalls Fan Ju as a Wei exile.

translations.literal: Cai Ze was a commoner from east of the mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Cai Ze was a plain man from Shandong.

sentences.zh: 有談范﹑蔡於許﹑史之閒，則狂矣。

translations.literal: To hold forth on Fan and Cai in the halls of the Xu and Shi clans would be madness.
translations.idiomatic: To lecture on Fan and Cai among the Xu and Shi favorites would be folly.

translations.literal: " The Play of the Guest that Gu wrote is treated in the Former Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu Ke xi is documented in the Han shu.

sentences.zh: 注[三]續漢志曰：「宮掖門，每門司馬一人，秩比千石。

translations.literal: Note [three] The Later Han Treatise says: "At each palace gate there is one marshal, rank equivalent to one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Xu Han zhi assigns a gate marshal to each inner gate.

translations.literal: The Black Tortoise marshal commands the Black Tortoise Gate."
translations.idiomatic: The Black Tortoise marshal holds the north gate.

sentences.zh: 注[四]章帝建初四年，詔諸王諸儒會白虎觀講議五經同異。

translations.literal: Note [four] In Zhangdi Jianchu 4 the edict summoned kings and scholars to the White Tiger Hall to debate agreements and differences in the Five Classics.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 4 brought lords and scholars to the White Tiger symposium.

sentences.zh: 時北單于遣使貢獻，求欲和親，詔問腢僚。

translations.literal: At that time the Northern Chanyu sent envoys with tribute, seeking peace through marriage; an edict asked the host of officials.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Shanyu offered tribute and asked for heqin; the court polled its ministers.

sentences.zh: 議者或以為「匈奴變詐之國，無內向之心，徒以畏漢威靈，逼憚南虜，[一]故希望報命，以安其離叛。

translations.literal: Some debaters thought: "The Xiongnu are a deceitful state without hearts turned inward; they only fear Han majesty and are coerced by dread of the southern foe, [one] therefore hoping for a return edict to steady their wavering defection.
translations.idiomatic: Some said the nomads were treacherous, inwardly hostile, only bowing from fear of Han and pressure from the Southern Xiongnu, seeking a reply to calm their splintering.

sentences.zh: 今若遣使，恐失南虜親附之歡，而成北狄猜詐之計，不可」。

translations.literal: If we now send envoys, we risk losing the southern tribes glad allegiance and fulfilling the northern Di suspicious stratagem — we must not."
translations.idiomatic: Sending envoys might alienate the south and reward northern cunning — they said no.

sentences.zh: 固議曰：「竊自惟思，漢興已來，曠世歷年，兵纏夷狄，尤事匈奴。

translations.literal: Gu argued: "I have privately reflected that since Han arose, ages and years have passed, and weapons have bound us to the Yi and Di, especially the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu argued that for generations Han had wrestled with barbarians, above all the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 綏御之方，其塗不一，或修文以和之，或用武以征之，或卑下以就之，[二]或臣服而致之。

translations.literal: The methods of pacifying and controlling them have not been one: some cultivated culture to harmonize them, some used arms to chastise them, some lowered themselves to accommodate them, [two] some received their submission to draw them in.
translations.idiomatic: Peace took many forms: culture, war, humble concession, or accepting submission.

sentences.zh: [三]雖屈申無常，所因時異，然未有拒絕□放，不與交接者也。

translations.literal: [Three] Though bending and stretching were never fixed and the times differed, never yet has any court refused them, cast them off, and refused all contact.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Policy flexed with the age, yet no dynasty ever simply cut the nomads off.

sentences.zh: 故自建武之世，復修舊典，數出重使，前後相繼，[四]至於其末，始乃暫絕。

translations.literal: Hence from the Jianwu era the old statutes were restored, heavy embassies went out again and again in succession, [four] until near the end there was only a brief break.
translations.idiomatic: From Jianwu Han renewed old embassies in steady succession until a short lapse late in the period.

translations.literal: In Yongping 8 the court again debated opening relations.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 8 reopened the question of contact.

sentences.zh: 而廷爭連日，異同紛回，多執其難，少言其易。

translations.literal: Yet court debate ran for days on end, agreement and difference wheeling back and forth; many grasped the difficulties, few spoke of the ease.
translations.idiomatic: Court quarreled for days, split on the issue, stressing risks over gains.

sentences.zh: 先帝聖德遠覽，瞻前顧後，遂復出使，事同前世。

translations.literal: The late emperor, sage in virtue and far-sighted, looked before and behind, and again sent envoys — the affair matched former ages.
translations.idiomatic: The late Mingdi looked ahead and behind and sent envoys again, as in old times.

sentences.zh: [五]以此而推，未有一世闕而不修者也。

translations.literal: [Five] From this one may infer: there has been no single age that broke off relations and did not repair them.
translations.idiomatic: [5] No generation simply abandoned the relationship forever.

sentences.zh: 今烏桓就闕，稽首譯官，康居﹑月氏，自遠而至，匈奴離析，名王來降，三方歸服，不以兵威，此誠國家通於神明自然之征也。

translations.literal: Now the Wuhuan attend at the palace gates, kowtow at the interpreters office; Kangju and the Yuezhi have come from afar; the Xiongnu have split apart and famous kings have surrendered — three quarters submit without the awe of arms. This is truly a sign that the state communicates with the spirits and with nature.
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan wait at the gates, Kangju and Yuezhi travel far, Xiongnu lords defect — three sides yield without war: a sign that Han moves heaven and nature.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為宜依故事，復遣使者，上可繼五鳳﹑甘露至遠人之會，[六]下不失建武﹑永平羈縻之義。

translations.literal: In my foolish opinion we should follow precedent and again dispatch envoys — above, we may continue the gathering of distant peoples as in Wufeng and Ganlu, [six] below, we would not lose the meaning of loose rein maintained in Jianwu and Yongping.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu urged restoring envoys to match Wufeng and Ganlu while keeping Jianwu-Yongping restraint.

sentences.zh: 虜使再來，然後一往，既明中國主在忠信，且知聖朝禮義有常，豈*

translations.literal: Let the barbarian envoys come twice, then we go once — thus we show that the Middle Kingdom rests on good faith, and that the sage court rites have constancy — how *
translations.idiomatic: Let them visit twice before we answer once, proving good faith and steady ritual — how

translations.literal: (tong)
translations.idiomatic: (then)

translations.literal: **can* we meet them with reverse suspicion, cutting off their good intent?"
translations.idiomatic: **could** we answer only with suspicion and wound their good will?"

sentences.zh: 絕之未知其利，通之不聞其害。

translations.literal: To cut them off — I have not heard its benefit; to open traffic — I have not heard its harm.
translations.idiomatic: Severance brings no clear gain; contact brings no clear harm.

sentences.zh: 設後北虜稍強，能為風塵，[七]方復求為交通，將何所及？

translations.literal: If later the northern foe grows somewhat strong and can raise wind and dust, [seven] then again seek intercourse — what good will that do?
translations.idiomatic: If the north grows strong and raids again, what leverage will we have if we have already spurned them?

sentences.zh: 不若因今施惠，為策近長。」

translations.literal: Better to extend favor now — that policy is nearer and longer-lasting."
translations.idiomatic: Better to show grace now; that is the nearer, longer policy.

translations.literal: Note [one]: The southern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: The southern Shanyu court.

sentences.zh: 注[二]文帝與匈奴通關市，妻以漢女，增厚其賂也。

translations.literal: Note [two]: Emperor Wen opened border markets with the Xiongnu, gave them Han wives in marriage, and greatly increased their gifts.
translations.idiomatic: Wendi traded, married princesses northward, and piled on gifts.

sentences.zh: 注[三]宣帝時，匈奴稽首臣服，遣子入侍。

translations.literal: Note [three]: Under Emperor Xuan the Xiongnu kowtowed in submission and sent a son as hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Xuandi saw the Shanyu submit and send a hostage prince.

sentences.zh: 注[四]建武二年，日逐王遣使詣漁陽請和親，使中郎將李茂報命。

translations.literal: Note [four]: In Jianwu 2 the Rizhu king sent envoys to Yuyang seeking heqin; Gentleman of the Palace Li Mao carried back the reply.
translations.idiomatic: Jianwu 2: Rizhu envoys to Yuyang; Li Mao returned the court answer.

sentences.zh: 二十六年，遣中郎將段郴授南單于印綬。

translations.literal: In year 26 Gentleman of the Palace Duan Chen invested the Southern Chanyu with seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Year 26: Duan Chen bestowed the southern seal.

translations.literal: Note [five]: The late emperor means Emperor Ming.
translations.idiomatic: Late emperor is Mingdi.

sentences.zh: 永平八年，遣越騎司馬鄭觿報使北匈奴。

translations.literal: In Yongping 8 Colonel of Swift Cavalry Zheng Zhong was sent as envoy in return to the Northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 8: Zheng Zhong answered the northern embassy.

sentences.zh: 注[六]宣帝五鳳三年，單于名王將觿五萬餘人來降，稱臣朝賀。

translations.literal: Note [six]: In Xuandi Wufeng 3 a famous Shanyu king led more than fifty thousand to surrender, declared himself a subject, and came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Wufeng 3: a royal title-holder brought fifty thousand to court.

sentences.zh: 甘露元年，匈奴呼韓邪遣子右賢王入侍。

translations.literal: In Ganlu 1 Chanyu Huhanye sent his son the Wise King of the Right as hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Ganlu 1: Huhanye sent the right wise king as hostage.

translations.literal: Note [seven]: When states invade each other, wind and dust rise.
translations.idiomatic: Border raids stir dust on the wind.

translations.literal: Gu also composed the Pattern and Pull piece, narrating Han virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu also wrote the Dian yin to hymn Han power.

sentences.zh: [一]以為相如封禪，靡而不典，[二]楊雄美新，典而不實，[三]蓋自謂得其致焉。

translations.literal: [One] He held that Sima Xiangru Feng and Shan rite was ornate but not canonical, [two] that Yang Xiong Praise the New was canonical but untrue, [three] thinking he himself had grasped the true balance.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He judged Xiangru rhapsody florid but not classical, Yang Praise to Xin classical but hollow, claiming his own piece hit the mark.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰： 注[一]典謂堯典，引猶續也。

translations.literal: Its text runs: Note [one]: Dian means the Canon of Yao; yin means to continue.
translations.idiomatic: The preface: dian is the Yao dian; yin means continuation.

sentences.zh: 漢承堯後，故述漢德以續堯典。

translations.literal: Han succeeds Yao; therefore narrating Han virtue continues the Canon of Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Han follows Yao, so hymn to Han extends the Yao dian.

sentences.zh: 注[二]文雖靡麗，而體無古典。

translations.literal: Note [two]: The language is ornate, yet the form lacks classical restraint.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru is lush but not canonical in form.

sentences.zh: 注[三]體雖典則，而其事虛偽，謂王莽事不實。

translations.literal: Note [three]: The form is canonical, yet the events are false — referring to the Wang Mang affair as untrue.
translations.idiomatic: Yang piece is formal but celebrates usurping Xin as fact.

sentences.zh: 太極之原，兩儀始分，鞭鞭熅熅，有沉而奧，有浮而清。

translations.literal: At the root of the Supreme Ultimate the two modes first divided — murky and dense, some sinking dark, some floating clear.
translations.idiomatic: From the Taiji the two modes split: turbid sinking, clear rising.

translations.literal: [One] Sinking and floating crossed and mingled; the myriad kinds blended into being.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Dark and light mingled; the myriad creatures took shape.

sentences.zh: [二]□命人主，五德初始，同於草昧，玄混之中。

translations.literal: [Two] Heaven commanded the lords of men; the five powers began their first turn, together in the grass-dark, in the black chaos.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Heaven ordained rulers; the five phases began their cycle in primal dusk and chaos.

sentences.zh: [三]踰繩越契，寂寥而亡詔者，系不得而綴也。

translations.literal: [Three] Beyond knotted cord and notched tally, silent and void without edicts — the appended texts could not string them.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Before writing, silence without proclamations — the Changes cannot bind that age.

sentences.zh: [四]厥有氏號，紹天闡繹者，[五]莫不開元於大昊皇初之首，上哉敻乎，其書猶可得而修也。

translations.literal: [Four] Then there were clan names and titles, those who continued Heaven and opened the line, [five] none but opened their primordial era at the head of Grand Haos royal dawn — how lofty! Yet their books may still be arranged.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Then came named houses that continued heaven, [5] each opening its era at Haos first dawn; high antiquity, yet still recordable.

sentences.zh: [六]亞斯之世，通變神化，函光而未曜。

translations.literal: [Six] In the age next after this, penetrating change and spirit transformation held light wrapped and not yet displayed.
translations.idiomatic: [6] The next ages shifted like spirits, their light still veiled.

translations.literal: [Seven]
translations.idiomatic: Seventh section marker in the cosmogonic prelude.

sentences.zh: 注[一]易系詞曰：「易有太極，是生兩儀。

translations.literal: Note [one] The Changes, "Appended Remarks" says: "Change has the Supreme Ultimate; it produces the two modes.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: Zhou yi on Taiji birthing yin and yang.

translations.literal: " It also says: "Heaven and earth intertwine; the myriad things transform and mature.
translations.idiomatic: " The Zhou yi again: heaven and earth mingle and things ripen.

translations.literal: " Cai Yong says: "*
translations.idiomatic: " Cai Yong glosses:

translations.literal: (bian)
translations.idiomatic: (intertwined)

translations.literal: **[yin]*yun — the appearance of yin and yang harmonized and supporting each other.
translations.idiomatic: **yin-yun** as yin and yang breathing together.

translations.literal: " Ao means turbid.
translations.idiomatic: " Ao means murky depth.

sentences.zh: 易干鑿度曰：「清輕者為天，濁沉者為地。」

translations.literal: The Changes, Qian zao du says: "The clear and light became heaven; the turbid and heavy became earth."
translations.idiomatic: The Qian zao du says light rose as heaven, heavy sank as earth.

translations.literal: Note [two]: Shu lei means the myriad things.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the myriad kinds.

translations.literal: Hun means the same as together.
translations.idiomatic: Hun means fused as one.

sentences.zh: 老子曰：「有物混成，先天地生。」

translations.literal: Laozi says: "There was a thing complete in chaos before heaven and earth were born."
translations.idiomatic: Laozi speaks of the chaos before heaven and earth.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Ren zhu means the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: the human lord is the sovereign.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰，成湯簡代夏作人主。

translations.literal: The Documents says that Cheng Tang selected the house of Xia to become lord of men.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu says Tang replaced Xia as ruler.

translations.literal: Wu de means the five phases.
translations.idiomatic: Wu de is the five agents.

sentences.zh: 初始謂伏犧始以木德王也。

translations.literal: Chu shi means Fuxi first ruled by the power of wood.
translations.idiomatic: The cycle begins with Fuxi as wood.

translations.literal: Wood produces fire, therefore Shennong ruled by the power of fire.
translations.idiomatic: Wood begets fire, so Shennong took fire.

translations.literal: The five phases produce one another, turning and beginning again.
translations.idiomatic: The agents generate each other in endless turn.

translations.literal: Cao mei means grass-creation and dimness.
translations.idiomatic: Cao mei is rude first beginnings.

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Heaven and earth were grass-dark.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou yi calls earliest heaven-earth cao mei.

sentences.zh: 」幽玄混沌之中謂三皇初起之時也。

translations.literal: " In the dark, mysterious chaos — that speaks of the first rise of the Three August Ones.
translations.idiomatic: " That is the twilight before the Three Sovereigns.

sentences.zh: 注[四]易系辭曰：「上古結繩而化，後代聖人易之以書契。

translations.literal: Note [four] The Changes, "Appended Remarks" says: "In high antiquity they knotted cords to govern; later sages replaced them with writing and contracts.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: from knotted cords to script.

translations.literal: Yu and yue both mean to pass beyond.
translations.idiomatic: Yu and yue both mean surpassing.

translations.literal: Zhao means proclamations.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao means edicts.

sentences.zh: 言過繩契以上既無文字，故寂寥而無文誥。

translations.literal: It means that beyond knotted cord and tally there was no writing, therefore silent and without written edicts.
translations.idiomatic: Before writing there could be no written decrees.

sentences.zh: 系謂易系辭也，故易系而不得綴連也。

translations.literal: Xi means the appended remarks on the Changes; therefore the Changes appendices could not link them.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi zhuan cannot tie that age into its text.

sentences.zh: 注[五]氏號謂太昊號庖羲氏，炎帝號神農氏，黃帝號軒轅氏之類。

translations.literal: Note [five]: Shi hao means that Grand Hao was titled Paoxi clan, the Flame Emperor Shennong clan, the Yellow Emperor Xuanyuan clan, and so on.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: clan names like Paoxi, Shennong, Xuanyuan.

translations.literal: Shao means to continue.
translations.idiomatic: Shao means to carry on.

translations.literal: It means kings who continued Heaven and undertook the work.
translations.idiomatic: Kings who acted as Heaven successors.

translations.literal: Chan means to open.
translations.idiomatic: Chan means to disclose.

translations.literal: Yi means to set forth.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means to unfold.

translations.literal: Note [six] The Changes says: "The emperor comes forth from the zhen trigram.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: the Yi says the emperor emerges from zhen.

sentences.zh: 」始以木德王天下，故曰皇初之首。

translations.literal: " He first ruled the realm by the power of wood; hence it is called the head of the royal dawn.
translations.idiomatic: " Wood was the first royal virtue, the start of the royal dawn.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「古者庖犧氏之王天下也，仰則觀象於天，俯則觀法於地。

translations.literal: It also says: "In antiquity Paoxi ruled the realm; looking up he observed images in heaven, looking down he observed models on earth.
translations.idiomatic: " The Yi praises Paoxi reading heaven and earth.

translations.literal: " Therefore their books can still be edited.
translations.idiomatic: " Hence their deeds can still be compiled.

sentences.zh: 注[七]亞斯之代謂少昊﹑顓頊﹑高辛等。

translations.literal: Note [seven]: The generation next after this means Shaohao, Zhuanxu, Gaoxin, and the like.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: the following generations are Shaohao onward.

sentences.zh: 雖通變神化，而易系不載其事，故曰「函光未曜」。

translations.literal: Though they penetrated change and spirit transformation, the appended remarks of the Changes do not record their deeds; hence it says "wrapped light not yet displayed."
translations.idiomatic: They transformed like spirits, yet the Yi zhuan omits them — light still hidden.

sentences.zh: 若夫上稽干則，降承龍翼，而炳諸典謨，以冠德卓蹤者，莫崇乎陶唐。

translations.literal: As for those who on high examined the stem norm, below received the dragon wings, and shone in the canons and counsels, crowning virtue and surpassing tracks — none was more honored than Tao Tang.
translations.idiomatic: Those who matched heaven above, drew ministers like dragon wings below, and wrote their virtue into the canons — none ranks above Yao of Tao Tang.

sentences.zh: [一]陶唐捨胤而禪有虞，虞亦命夏後，稷契熙載，越成湯武。

translations.literal: [One] Tao Tang yielded the heir and abdicated to Shun of Yu; Yu in turn charged the lord of Xia; Ji and Qi shone in their tasks, crossing to accomplished Tang and Wu.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yao yielded to Shun, Shun to Yu, Yu to Xia; Ji and Qi served until Tang and Wu rose.

sentences.zh: [二]股肱既周，天乃歸功元首，將授漢劉。

translations.literal: [Two] When the arms and thighs were complete, Heaven returned the merit to the first head and was about to invest the house of Liu of Han.
translations.idiomatic: [2] When ministers had done their work, heaven turned merit to the true head and prepared the Liu mandate.

sentences.zh: [三]俾其承三季之荒末，值亢龍之災孽，懸象暗而恆文乖，彝倫斁而舊章缺。

translations.literal: [Three] To make them inherit the wasteland end of three final ages, meet the calamity of the soaring dragon at its height, while hanging images grew dark and constant patterns went awry, and the great norms collapsed and old statutes were missing.
translations.idiomatic: [3] They would take up the ruin of three decadent endings, the disaster of the top line dragon, omens dark and rites broken.

sentences.zh: [四]故先命玄聖，使綴學立制，宏亮洪業，表相祖宗，讚揚迪哲，備哉燦爛，真神明之式也。

translations.literal: [Four] Therefore Heaven first charged the dark sage to stitch learning and set institutions, to magnify and brighten the great enterprise, to display the ancestors, to praise wise continuity — complete and radiant, truly the model of gods and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: [4] So heaven first sent the dark sage to fix learning and law, glorify the great work, frame the ancestors — a pattern fit for gods.

sentences.zh: [五]雖前*[聖]*戲﹑夔﹑衡﹑旦密勿之輔，比茲褊矣。

translations.literal: [Five] Though former sages Xi, Kui, Heng, and Dan were close helpers in tireless vigil, compared with this they were narrow.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Even the tireless ministers Xi, Kui, Yi Yin, and the Duke of Zhou pale beside this charge.

sentences.zh: [六]是以高﹑光二聖，辰居其域，時至氣動，乃龍見淵躍。

translations.literal: [Six] Therefore the two sages Gao and Guang sat like stars in their domain; when the season arrived and vapor stirred, then the dragon was seen leaping in the deep.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Gaozu and Guangwu sat like polar stars; when the hour came, the dragon leapt from the pool.

sentences.zh: [七]拊翼而未舉，則威靈紛紜，海內雲蒸，雷動電熛，胡縊莽分，不馬其誅。

translations.literal: [Seven] While they folded wings and had not yet risen, awesome numen swirled in confusion; within the seas it steamed like clouds, thunder moved and lightning flashed — the Hu were broken, Mang split apart, before the horses of their punishments could run.
translations.idiomatic: [7] Before they struck, awe filled the realm like storm clouds; northern tribes collapsed, Wang Mang shattered, all before the war chariots rolled.

sentences.zh: [八]然後欽若上下，恭揖腢後，正位度宗，有於德不台淵穆之讓，靡號師矢敦奮撝之容。

translations.literal: [Eight] Then they reverently accorded with high and low, bowed to the host of ministers, took the correct place and measured the lineage temple, showing the yielding modesty of one whose virtue did not match the deep silence — without boastful cries or brandished spear-waving mien.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Then they harmonized court and country, yielded to ministers, took the throne with modest disclaimers, without vain war-cries.

sentences.zh: [九]蓋以膺當天之正統，受克讓之歸運，蓄炎上之烈精，蘊孔佐之弘陳云爾。

translations.literal: [Nine] In sum, because they bore the orthodox mandate of matching heaven, received the returning fortune of mastered yielding, stored the fierce essence of flaming ascendance, and harbored the great array of Confucian helpers — thus it was.
translations.idiomatic: [9] They held heaven true mandate, received abdicated fortune, kept Han fire essence, and drew on Confucian ministers — so it stood.

translations.literal: [Ten] Note [one]: Ji means examine;
translations.idiomatic: [10] Commentary [1]: ji is to examine;

translations.literal: gan means heaven.
translations.idiomatic: gan means heaven.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子曰：「唯天為大，唯堯則之。

translations.literal: The Analects records Confucius as saying: "Great indeed is Yao as a ruler! Only Heaven is great, and only Yao patterned himself on it.
translations.idiomatic: The Analects praises Yao as matching heaven greatness.

translations.literal: " Long wings means Ji and Qi and the like as Yao wings.
translations.idiomatic: " Dragon wings are ministers like Ji and Qi supporting Yao.

sentences.zh: 易幹上九曰：「用九，見腢龍無首，吉。

translations.literal: The Changes, qian line for use of nine says: "Use of nine — seeing many dragons without a head — auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: The qian use-of-nine line speaks of many dragons headless, good fortune.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「六爻皆體龍，腢龍象也，謂禹與稷﹑契﹑咎陶之屬並在於朝。
