translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "All six lines embody the dragon; many dragons is the image — meaning Yu together with Ji, Qi, Gao Yao, and the like were all at court."
translations.idiomatic: " Zheng Xuan reads the many dragons as Yu, Ji, Qi, and Gao Yao serving together."

translations.literal: " Bing means bright.
translations.idiomatic: " Bing means shining clarity.

translations.literal: Dian and mo mean the Canon of Yao and the Counsels of Gao Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Dian and mo are the Yao dian and Gao Yao mo.

sentences.zh: 為道德之冠首，蹤多之卓異者，莫高於陶唐。

translations.literal: As the crown of the Way and its virtue, the most outstanding of many tracks — none was higher than Tao Tang.
translations.idiomatic: As the summit of moral pattern, the noblest of many paths — none rose above Yao of Tao Tang.

translations.literal: The Erya says: "Chong means high."
translations.idiomatic: The Erya glosses chong as high.

sentences.zh: 注[二]捨胤謂堯捨其胤子丹朱而禪於舜，舜亦捨其子商均而禪禹。

translations.literal: Note [two]: She yin means that Yao set aside his heir Dan Zhu and abdicated to Shun, and Shun likewise set aside his son Shang Jun and abdicated to Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: yielding heirs — Yao passed Shun over Dan Zhu, Shun passed Yu over Shang Jun.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "They brightened the emperor undertakings.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu says they widened the sovereign work.

translations.literal: " Kong Anguo commentary says: "Xi means to extend.
translations.idiomatic: " Kong Anguo: xi means to broaden.

translations.literal: Zai means affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Zai means tasks or affairs.

sentences.zh: 」言稷契並能廣立功事於堯舜之朝。

translations.literal: " It means Ji and Qi together could extend meritorious service at the courts of Yao and Shun.
translations.idiomatic: " Ji and Qi both enlarged the work of Yao and Shun courts.

translations.literal: Yue means "thereupon."
translations.idiomatic: Yue is "thereupon" or "then."

sentences.zh: 於是成其子孫湯﹑武之業，並得為天子也。

translations.literal: Thereby they completed the enterprises of their descendants Tang and Wu, both attaining the position of Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: So their lines rose to Tang and Wu as Sons of Heaven.

translations.literal: Tang was descended from Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Tang came from the Qi line.

translations.literal: King Wu was descended from Hou Ji.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu came from Hou Ji line.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Gu gong means Ji and Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: arms and thighs are Ji and Qi.

sentences.zh: 既周謂其子孫並周□得為天子。

translations.literal: Ji zhou means their descendants all in turn each attained the throne as Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Ji zhou means their heirs in turn each took the throne.

translations.literal: Yuan shou means Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan shou is Yao, the first head.

sentences.zh: 言天更歸功於堯，又將授漢以帝位。

translations.literal: It means Heaven again returned the merit to Yao and was about to invest Han with the imperial seat.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven credited Yao again, then passed the seat to Han.

translations.literal: Note [four]: Bi means to cause.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: bi means "to make."

translations.literal: San ji means the ends of the three kings.
translations.idiomatic: The three ji are the late ages of the three dynasties.

sentences.zh: 易干文言曰：「亢龍有悔，窮之災也。

translations.literal: The Changes, Qian wen yan says: "The soaring dragon will have regrets — the disaster of exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: The Qian wen yan warns the top-line dragon brings regret.

translations.literal: " Nie also means calamity.
translations.idiomatic: " Nie means disaster too.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「懸象著明，莫大於日月。

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Suspended images revealing brightness — nothing is greater than sun and moon.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi says hanging images shine brightest in sun and moon.

translations.literal: " Guai means losing the constant measure.
translations.idiomatic: Guai is departure from the norm.

translations.literal: Lun means pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Lun is moral order.

translations.literal: Yi means ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means collapse.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The constant norms were ruined.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu says the great norms failed.

translations.literal: " Old statutes missing means Qin burning the Odes and Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Missing statutes means the Qin book-burning.

translations.literal: Note [five]: Xuan sheng means Kong Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: the dark sage is Confucius.

sentences.zh: 春秋演孔圖曰：「孔子母征在夢感黑帝而生，故曰玄聖。

translations.literal: The Chunqiu yan Kong tu says: "Confucius mother Zheng Zai dreamed she was touched by the Black Thearch and bore him; hence he is called the dark sage.
translations.idiomatic: The apocryphon says Confucius was born of a black god dream.

translations.literal: " Zhuangzi says: "Still and silent is the way of the dark sage, the king without a throne."
translations.idiomatic: Zhuangzi calls him the unthroned king.

translations.literal: Stitching learning and setting institutions means establishing Han house laws and models.
translations.idiomatic: He set learning and institutions for the Han code.

translations.literal: Hong and hong both mean great.
translations.idiomatic: Hong and hong both mean vast.

translations.literal: Liang means faithful.
translations.idiomatic: Liang means trustworthy.

translations.literal: Biao means to make clear.
translations.idiomatic: Biao means to display.

translations.literal: Xiang means to assist.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang means to help.

translations.literal: Di means to tread.
translations.idiomatic: Di means to walk or follow.

translations.literal: Zhe means wise.
translations.idiomatic: Zhe means wisdom.

sentences.zh: 言讚揚蹈履哲智之君，謂高祖等也。

translations.literal: It means praising and treading after wise rulers — referring to Gaozu and the like.
translations.idiomatic: He hymns wise rulers such as Gaozu.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "These four men followed the wise course.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu praises four wise ministers.

translations.literal: Can lan means blazingly clear.
translations.idiomatic: Can lan is radiant clarity.

translations.literal: Shi means model.
translations.idiomatic: Shi means pattern or model.

translations.literal: Note [six]: Xi means Gao Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: Xi is Gao Yao.

translations.literal: Kui was the one who managed music for Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Kui directed music under Shun.

translations.literal: Heng means the balancing minister — that is Yi Yin.
translations.idiomatic: Heng is Yi Yin, the Ah Heng.

translations.literal: Dan is the Duke of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Dan is the Duke of Zhou.

translations.literal: Mi wu is the same as min mian — striving.
translations.idiomatic: Mi wu means tireless effort.

sentences.zh: 茲謂孔子，言戲﹑夔等比之為褊小矣。

translations.literal: Zi refers to Confucius; it means that Xi, Kui, and the others, compared to him, were narrow.
translations.idiomatic: Compared to Confucius, even Xi and Kui seem small.

sentences.zh: 注[七]論語孔子曰：「譬如北辰，居其所而觿星共之。

translations.literal: Note [seven] The Analects records Confucius as saying: "He is like the north star, dwelling in his place while the many stars pay him court.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: the Analects north-star image.

sentences.zh: 」時至氣動謂高祖聚彤雲於碭山，光武發佳氣於白水。

translations.literal: " "When the season arrives and vapor stirs" means Gaozu gathering crimson clouds at Dang Mountain, Guangwu sending auspicious vapor at Baishui.
translations.idiomatic: " Omens gathered at Dang for Gaozu, at Baishui for Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 易干卦九二曰：「見龍在田。

translations.literal: The Changes, Qian hexagram line two says: "The dragon appears in the field.
translations.idiomatic: The Qian second line: dragon in the field.

translations.literal: " Line four says: "Or leaping in the deep.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth line: the dragon may leap in the pool.

translations.literal: " Both metaphorically describe the early rise of Han.
translations.idiomatic: Both lines image the early Han rise.

sentences.zh: 注[八]拊翼，以雞為喻，言知將旦則鼓其翼而鳴。

translations.literal: Note [eight]: Fu yi uses the cock as metaphor — knowing dawn is near it beats its wings and crows.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [8]: fu yi compares Han founders to a cock before dawn.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「張﹑陳之交，拊翼俱起。

translations.literal: The Former Documents says: "At the friendship of Zhang and Chen they beat their wings and rose together.
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu says Zhang and Chen roused together.

translations.literal: " It is used to metaphor Gaozu and Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: The image fits Gaozu and Guangwu.

translations.literal: Fen yun describes a flourishing appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Fen yun is teeming splendor.

sentences.zh: 如雲之蒸，言天下英傑為漢者多也。

translations.literal: Like clouds steaming — it means heroes of the realm flocked to Han in great numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Heroes steamed toward Han like clouds.

translations.literal: Biao means light.
translations.idiomatic: Biao means lightning-flash.

translations.literal: Hu yi means Hu Hai strangling himself.
translations.idiomatic: Hu yi is the suicide of the Second Emperor.

translations.literal: Mang fen means Gongbin Jiu beheading Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Mang fen is Gongbin Jiu killing Wang Mang.

translations.literal: Ma means to approach.
translations.idiomatic: Ma means to attend in person.

sentences.zh: 言天下先為漢誅之，高祖﹑光武不親臨也。

translations.literal: It means the realm first executed them for Han; Gaozu and Guangwu did not personally approach.
translations.idiomatic: The empire destroyed Qin and Xin before the two founders needed to strike.

translations.literal: Note [nine] The Documents says: "Reverently accord with August Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu bids reverent accord with heaven.

translations.literal: " Qin means reverent.
translations.idiomatic: " Qin means respectful.

translations.literal: Ruo means to follow.
translations.idiomatic: Ruo means to obey.

sentences.zh: 上下謂天地也，書曰「格於上下」。

translations.literal: Shang xia means Heaven and earth; the Documents says "penetrating to high and low."
translations.idiomatic: High and low are heaven and earth, as in the Shu phrase.

translations.literal: Zhong hou means the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong hou are the regional lords.

translations.literal: The Changes says: "The gentleman corrects his position and firms the mandate."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi says the gentleman fixes his seat and steadies the charge.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「延入翼室恤度宗。

translations.literal: The Documents says: "He delayed entering the wing chamber to care for the honored lineage head.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu describes yielding before the lineage elder.

translations.literal: " Du means to dwell.
translations.idiomatic: " Du means to reside.

translations.literal: Zong means honored.
translations.idiomatic: Zong means venerable.

translations.literal: The Former Documents says: "Shun yielded because his virtue did not match the succession."
translations.idiomatic: The Han shu quotes Shun modest disclaimer.

translations.literal: " The Yin-yi says: "Tai should be read si (succession)."
translations.idiomatic: Tai is read like si, succession.

sentences.zh: 注[一０]正統謂漢承周，為火德。

translations.literal: Note [ten]: Zheng tong means Han succeeding Zhou as the power of fire.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [10]: orthodox Han fire after Zhou.

translations.literal: The Canon of Yao says: "Truly respectful, able to yield.
translations.idiomatic: The Yao dian praises yielding virtue.

translations.literal: " It means Han succeeds Yao in mastered yielding.
translations.idiomatic: " Han continues Yao tradition of yielding.

translations.literal: Gui yun means Yao returning the revolving fortune to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Gui yun is the turning mandate passed to Han.

sentences.zh: 炎上謂火德，烈精言盛也。

translations.literal: Yan shang means the power of fire; lie jing speaks of its blazing intensity.
translations.idiomatic: Yan shang is fire virtue blazing bright.

translations.literal: Yun means to store.
translations.idiomatic: Yun means to harbor.

sentences.zh: 孔佐謂孔丘製作春秋及緯書以佐漢也，即春秋演孔圖曰「卯金刀，名為劉，中國東南出荊州，赤帝后，次代周」是也，謂大陳漢之期運也。

translations.literal: Kong zuo means Kong Qiu composing the Spring and Autumn and weft texts to assist Han — as the Chunqiu yan Kong tu says: "The metal blade mao named Liu, arising in the southeast of China from Jingzhou, descendant of the Red Thearch, next after Zhou" — that is, broadly setting forth the destined season of Han.
translations.idiomatic: Kong zuo is Confucius texts prophesying the Liu house and Han season.

sentences.zh: 洋洋乎若德，帝者之上儀，誥誓所不及已。

translations.literal: How vast such virtue — the highest norm for emperors, beyond what proclamations and oaths can reach.
translations.idiomatic: Such virtue outruns mere oaths and edicts.

sentences.zh: [一]舖觀二代洪纖之度，其賾可探也。

translations.literal: [One] Spread to view the measures, great and small, of the two dynasties — their depth may be fathomed.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Survey Yin and Zhou, great and small — their depth yields to inquiry.

sentences.zh: [二]並開多於一匱，同受侯甸之所服，奕世勤民，以伯方統牧。

translations.literal: [Two] Both opened their bounty from a single coffer, alike received the service of marches and interior, age after age labored for the people, as elder lords they unified the shepherds.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Both drew from one treasury, ruled marches and interior, toiled for the folk, elder hegemons of the herdsmen.

sentences.zh: [三]乘其命賜彤弧黃戚之威，用討韋﹑顧﹑黎﹑崇之不格。

translations.literal: [Three] Riding their mandate they wielded the awes of cinnabar bow and yellow battle-axe, used them to punish Wei, Gu, Li, and Chong for not coming to court.
translations.idiomatic: [3] They bore red bow and golden axe to chastise recalcitrant lords.

sentences.zh: [四]至乎三五華夏，京遷鎬亳，遂自北面，虎離其師，革滅天邑。

translations.literal: [Four] Down to the three-five age of Hua-Xia, when the capital moved to Hao and Bo, then turning northward, the tiger left his hosts, by revolution destroying the great city of heaven.
translations.idiomatic: [4] Through the three-five sagely age, capitals shifted; Zhou turned north, broke Shang hosts, overthrew the heavenly city.

sentences.zh: [五]是故義士偉而不敦，武稱未盡，護有籩德，不其然與？

translations.literal: [Five] Therefore righteous men praised but did not press; martial fame was not exhausted; Hu had modest virtue — was it not so?
translations.idiomatic: [5] So worthies praised without pressing; war-song fell short; Hu had scant virtue — was it not thus?

sentences.zh: [六]然猶於穆猗那，翕純皦繹，以崇嚴祖考，殷薦宗祀配帝，發祥流慶，對越天地者，舄奕乎千載。

translations.literal: [Six] Yet still, how august, how fine — pure, bright, and continuous — thereby honoring stern ancestors, richly offering in lineage sacrifice to match High God, sending forth blessing and spreading celebration, answering Heaven and earth — shining across a thousand ages.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Still their hymns rang august, their sacrifice matched heaven, blessing flowed for ages.

translations.literal: Could they not themselves match the spirits and gods!
translations.idiomatic: Did they not match the gods themselves?

sentences.zh: [七]誕略有常，審言行於篇籍，光藻朗而不渝耳。

translations.literal: [Seven] Vast indeed are the enduring norms; examining words and deeds in the writings, their radiance stays bright and never fades.
translations.idiomatic: [7] Their constant pattern endures; in texts their light never dims.

translations.literal: [Eight]
translations.idiomatic: Eighth section marker in the Dian yin passage.

translations.literal: Note [one]: Yang yang means beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: yang yang is glorious fullness.

translations.literal: Ruo means "like."
translations.idiomatic: Ruo means like or as.

translations.literal: Yi means norm or law.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means model or law.

sentences.zh: 謂如此美德，可謂五帝之上法也。

translations.literal: It means that virtue such as this may be called the highest law of the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: Such virtue ranks as the Five Thearchs supreme pattern.

sentences.zh: 谷梁傳曰：「誥誓不及五帝，盟詛不及三王，交質不及二伯。

translations.literal: The Guliang Commentary says: "Proclamations and oaths did not reach the Five Thearchs; covenants and curses did not reach the Three Kings; exchange of hostages did not reach the two hegemons.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang zhuan says oaths stopped before the Five Thearchs.

translations.literal: " When high and low did not trust one another, then there were proclamations and oaths.
translations.idiomatic: " Oaths appear only when trust fails.

sentences.zh: 五帝之時，上下和睦，故誓不及。

translations.literal: In the time of the Five Thearchs, high and low were harmonious; therefore oaths did not reach them.
translations.idiomatic: Under the Five Thearchs harmony ruled, so oaths were needless.

translations.literal: Note [two]: Pu means to spread or display.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: pu means to lay out or survey.

translations.literal: Er dai means Yin and Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: The two dynasties are Shang and Zhou.

translations.literal: Hong xian means large and small.
translations.idiomatic: Hong xian means the great and the small scale.

translations.literal: Du means measure or standard.
translations.idiomatic: Du is norm or scale.

translations.literal: Ze means deep and hidden.
translations.idiomatic: Ze means abstruse depth.

sentences.zh: 言□觀殷周大小之法，其幽深可探知之。

translations.literal: It means that reviewing Yin and Zhou norms, great and small, their hidden depth can be explored.
translations.idiomatic: Surveying Shang and Zhou, their measure runs deep enough to sound.

sentences.zh: 注[三]孔子曰：「譬如平地，雖覆一匱。

translations.literal: Note [three] Confucius said: "It is like leveling ground — though you overturn only one basket.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius compares reform to leveling earth one basket at a time.

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "Kui is a basket for carrying earth.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: kui is a dirt hod.

translations.literal: " Hou fu and dian fu mean the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Hou and dian zones mean the regional lords.

sentences.zh: 湯為桀之諸侯，文王為紂之諸侯。

translations.literal: Tang was a lord under Jie; King Wen was a lord under Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Tang served Jie; Wen served the last Shang king.

translations.literal: Yi means repeated or layered.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means generation upon generation.

sentences.zh: 自契至湯十四代，後稷至文王十五代，並積勤勞於人也。

translations.literal: From Qi to Tang were fourteen generations; from Hou Ji to King Wen fifteen generations — both piled up toil for the people.
translations.idiomatic: Fourteen generations from Qi to Tang, fifteen from Hou Ji to Wen — long labor for the folk.

translations.literal: Bo fang is the same as fang bo.
translations.idiomatic: Bo fang equals regional elder.

sentences.zh: 謂湯為夏伯，文王為殷伯，並統領州牧。

translations.literal: It means Tang was elder to Xia and Wen was elder to Yin, both commanding the shepherds of the provinces.
translations.idiomatic: Tang was Xia fangbo, Wen was Yin fangbo, each leading provincial herdsmen.

translations.literal: Note [four] The Zhou li: nine grades of investiture make an elder.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou li sets nine ranks culminating in hegemon.

translations.literal: Tong hu is a cinnabar bow.
translations.idiomatic: The red bow of investiture.

translations.literal: Huang qi is an axe with gold ornament.
translations.idiomatic: The yellow battle-axe, gilded.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「諸侯賜弓矢然後專征伐，賜斧鉞然後殺。

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: "Feudal lords, only after receiving bow and arrows may they wage war alone; only after receiving axe and halberd may they execute.
translations.idiomatic: The Li ji ties campaign and execution rights to regalia.

translations.literal: " Wei and Gu are both state names; Tang destroyed them.
translations.idiomatic: Wei and Gu were realms Tang crushed.

translations.literal: The Yin hymn says: "Wei and Gu were already attacked.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang song lists Wei and Gu struck.

translations.literal: " Li and Chong are also state names.
translations.idiomatic: Li and Chong were further targets.

translations.literal: The Records: "King Wen attacked Chong."
translations.idiomatic: The Shi ji records Wen attacking Chong.

translations.literal: " The Documents says: "The western lord chastised Li."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu has the western earl reduce Li.

translations.literal: " Ge means to come.
translations.idiomatic: Ge means to present oneself at court.

translations.literal: Note [five] San wu — not detailed here.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: "three-five" sages left unspecified.

translations.literal: Jing *
translations.idiomatic: The capital

translations.literal: (shi)
translations.idiomatic: (metropolis)

translations.literal: * means the capital city.
translations.idiomatic: means the royal capital.

translations.literal: King Wu made his capital at Hao; Tang made his at Bo.
translations.idiomatic: Wu ruled from Hao, Tang from Bo.

sentences.zh: 詩云：「宅是鎬京，武王成之。

translations.literal: The Odes says: "He dwelt in this Hao capital; King Wu completed it."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes celebrate completing Hao.

translations.literal: " The Documents says: "Tang first dwelt at Bo, following the former kings."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu places Tang at Bo with the ancient kings.

translations.literal: " Zi means "from."
translations.idiomatic: Zi means hence or from.

translations.literal: Bei mian means serving as subject.
translations.idiomatic: Facing north means subject status.

translations.literal: Tang and Wu both used the status of subject to attack their lords.
translations.idiomatic: Both rose as vassals against their kings.

sentences.zh: 史記曰：「如虎如羆，如豺如離，於商郊。

translations.literal: The Records says: "Like tiger, like brown bear, like wolf, like li — at the outskirts of Shang."
translations.idiomatic: The Shi ji paints Zhou hosts at Shang border.

translations.literal: " The Yin-yi says: "Li is the same as chi."
translations.idiomatic: Li is read like chi, a hornless dragon.

translations.literal: Ge means change.
translations.idiomatic: Ge is revolution or change.

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Tang and Wu revolution."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi names the Tang-Wu revolution.

translations.literal: Tian yi means the city where the Son of Heaven dwells.
translations.idiomatic: Tian yi is the dynastic capital.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「肆予敢求爾於天邑商。」

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Therefore I dare to seek you in the great city of Shang of heaven."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu dares ask Shang in its heavenly city.

sentences.zh: 注[六]左傳曰：「武王克商，遷九鼎於洛邑，義士猶曰薄德。

translations.literal: Note [six] The Zuo Tradition says: "When Wu conquered Shang and moved the nine tripods to Luoyi, righteous men still called it scant virtue."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: the Zuo zhuan says even after moving tripods some called Zhou virtue thin.

translations.literal: " Du Yu commentary says: "Men like Boyi."
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu means Boyi sort of purists.

sentences.zh: 」史記曰，伯夷、叔齊逢武王伐紂，扣馬諫曰：「以臣弒君，可謂仁乎？

translations.literal: The Records says that Boyi and Shu Qi met King Wu attacking Zhou; they seized the horses and remonstrated: "To kill one's lord as a subject — can that be called humane?"
translations.idiomatic: Boyi and Shu Qi blocked Wu horse, asking if regicide is humane.

translations.literal: Wei means strange or unusual.
translations.idiomatic: Wei means singular or odd.

translations.literal: Dun means thick.
translations.idiomatic: Dun means substantial.

translations.literal: Wu is the music of King Wu of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Wu is Zhou martial dance.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子曰：「謂武盡美矣，未盡善也。

translations.literal: The Analects records Confucius as saying: "As for Wu, it reached full beauty, not yet full goodness."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius says the Wu dance is beautiful but not perfect good.

translations.literal: Hu is Tang music.
translations.idiomatic: Hu is Shang Tang dance music.

sentences.zh: 左傳，延陵季子聘魯，觀樂，見舞大護者，曰：「聖人之弘也，而猶有籩德。」

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition: when Prince Jizi of Yanling visited Lu and watched the dances, seeing the Great Hu he said: "The sage greatness — yet it still had scant virtue."
translations.idiomatic: Yanling in Zuo zhuan calls the Hu dance great yet flawed.

translations.literal: Note [seven]: Yu is a sighing particle.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: yu opens a praise.

translations.literal: Mu means beautiful — sighing praise for the house of Zhou virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Mu praises Zhou virtue.

translations.literal: The Zhou hymn says "Oh, how august the clear temple."
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou song has yu mu qing miao.

translations.literal: Yi is also a sigh *
translations.idiomatic: Yi is also an exclamation

translations.literal: (zhi)
translations.idiomatic: (of)

translations.literal: * particle.
translations.idiomatic: particle of praise.

translations.literal: Na means abundant.
translations.idiomatic: Na means many.

translations.literal: It sighs praise for the abundance of Tang virtue.
translations.idiomatic: It acclaims Tang virtue in full measure.

translations.literal: The Yin hymn says: "Oh, how grand! Ah, how fine!"
translations.idiomatic: The Shang song cries yi yu na yu.

sentences.zh: 」論語子語魯太師樂曰：「樂其可知也。

translations.literal: The Analects records the Master instructing the grand music master of Lu: "Music can be understood.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius teaches the Lu music master how music unfolds.

sentences.zh: 始作翕如也，縱之純如也，皦如也，繹如也，以成。

translations.literal: When it first begins, all breathe together; when released, it is pure and harmonious, bright and clear, continuous — thus it completes."
translations.idiomatic: It starts unified, opens pure, clear, flowing to the end.

translations.literal: " He Yan commentary says: "Xi means flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: He Yan: xi is swelling together.

translations.literal: Chun means harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: Chun is harmonious blend.

translations.literal: Jiao means its notes and rhythm are clear.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao is limpid phrasing.

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "Yi means the appearance of smooth penetration.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: yi is fluent carry-through.

sentences.zh: 」此言殷周之代，尚有於穆猗那之頌，播之於翕純皦繹之樂，尊祖嚴父，宗祀配天於明堂之中。

translations.literal: " This speaks of the Yin and Zhou ages still having songs of yu mu and yi na, broadcast in music that was xi, chun, jiao, and yi — honoring ancestors and stern fathers, lineage sacrifice matching High God within the Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and Zhou still sang august hymns in layered, clear music under the Ming tang.

sentences.zh: 詩商頌曰：「浚哲惟商，長髮其祥。

translations.literal: The Shang hymn says: "Profoundly wise was Shang; long did its omens unfold."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang song praises Shang foresight.

translations.literal: It means sending forth auspicious signs to pour blessing on descendants.
translations.idiomatic: Omens spread blessing down the line.

sentences.zh: 周頌曰：「秉文之德，對越在天。

translations.literal: The Zhou hymn says: "Holding King Wen pattern, answering heaven above."
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou song answers heaven with Wen virtue.

translations.literal: Xi yi means linked without break.
translations.idiomatic: Xi yi is unbroken succession.

translations.literal: Note [eight]: Dan means great.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [8]: dan means vast.

sentences.zh: 言殷週二代政化之多，大略有常也。

translations.literal: It means the Yin and Zhou two ages had abundant transformation, yet in broad outline they kept constancy.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and Zhou policy was rich yet held a steady thread.

translations.literal: Pian ji means the Odes and Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Pian ji is the classic texts.

translations.literal: Lang means bright.
translations.idiomatic: Lang means luminous.

translations.literal: Yu means change.
translations.idiomatic: Yu means to alter.

sentences.zh: 言光彩文藻朗明而不變耳，其餘殊異不能及於漢也。

translations.literal: It means their radiance and pattern stayed bright and unchanging — the rest of their oddities could not match Han.
translations.idiomatic: Their literary glory stayed fixed; still they fell short of Han.

sentences.zh: 矧夫赫赫聖漢，巍巍唐基，泝測其源，乃先孕虞育夏，甄殷陶周，[一]然後宣二祖之重光，襲四宗之緝熙。

translations.literal: How much more the blazing sage Han, the towering Tang foundation — tracing the source upstream, it first conceived Yu and nurtured Xia, molded Yin and shaped Zhou, [one] then displayed the doubled radiance of the two founders, inherited the continued splendor of four exalted temples.
translations.idiomatic: Far more Han, built on Tang base, bred from Yu and Xia, cast Shang and Zhou, then shone Gaozu and Guangwu, carried four temple lines.

sentences.zh: 神靈日燭，光被六幽，仁風翔乎海表，威靈行於鬼區，慝亡迥而不泯，微胡瑣而不頤。

translations.literal: [Two] Spirits and gods daily shine; light reaches the six darks; humane wind soars beyond the seas, awesome numen walks in ghost regions — evil nowhere so far it is not quenched, petty Hu nowhere so small it is not nurtured.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Gods illumine daily; kindness crosses seas, awe haunts far ghosts; wickedness dies however remote, tribes thrive however small.

sentences.zh: [二]故夫顯定三才昭登之績，匪堯不興，舖聞遺策在下之訓，匪漢不弘。

translations.literal: [Three] Therefore the manifest ordering of the three powers and the merit of bright ascent — none but Yao could raise it; spreading abroad the leftover plans and the teaching for those below — none but Han could enlarge it.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Heaven-earth-man order rose with Yao; Yao dian teaching grew vast only under Han.

sentences.zh: [三]厥道至乎經緯乾坤，出入三光，外運混元，內浸豪芒，性類循理，品物鹹亨，其已久矣。

translations.literal: [Four] Its way reaches to warp and weft of heaven and earth, to going forth and entering with the three luminaries, outwardly coursing the primal chaos, inwardly steeping the finest tip — natures and kinds follow pattern, the myriad things all thrive: long indeed has it been so.
translations.idiomatic: [4] It spans cosmos, rides sun moon stars, moves in chaos, wets the smallest mote — all kinds prosper: this has lasted ages.

translations.literal: [Four]
translations.idiomatic: Fourth numbered section in the source sequence.

translations.literal: Note [one]: Shen means "how much more."
translations.idiomatic: Shen is rhetorical "how much more."

translations.literal: Han inherits the Tang *
translations.idiomatic: Han stands on Tang

translations.literal: (Yu)
translations.idiomatic: (and Yu)

translations.literal: **[Yao]* foundation.
translations.idiomatic: **Yao** foundation.

translations.literal: Su means to go upstream.
translations.idiomatic: Su is tracing upstream.

translations.literal: Yun means to conceive.
translations.idiomatic: Yun means to quicken in the womb.

translations.literal: Yu means to rear.
translations.idiomatic: Yu means to nurture.

translations.literal: Zhen and tao mean to fashion and mold.
translations.idiomatic: Zhen and tao mean to shape like a potter.

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「陶人作瓦器謂之甄。

translations.literal: The Former Documents Yin-yi says: "The potter who makes vessels calls it zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Han shu gloss: potters zhen ware.

sentences.zh: 」言虞、夏、殷、周之先祖，並嘗為堯臣。

translations.literal: " It means the ancestors of Yu, Xia, Yin, and Zhou all once served as Yao ministers.
translations.idiomatic: All those dynastic forebears served Yao.

translations.literal: Note [two]: The two founders are Gaozu and Shizu.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: the two zu are Liu Bang and Guangwu.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "He displayed redoubled radiance."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu speaks of doubled brilliance.

translations.literal: Xi means to repeat or layer.
translations.idiomatic: Xi means to inherit in layers.

sentences.zh: 四宗，文帝為太宗，武帝為代宗，宣帝為中宗，明帝為顯宗。

translations.literal: The four exalted temples: Wendi is Taizong, Wudi is Daizong, Xuandi is Zhongzong, Mingdi is Xianzong.
translations.idiomatic: The four zong are Wen, Wu as Daizong, Xuan, Ming as Xian.

translations.literal: Zhu means to shine — it speaks of shining like the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu is sun-bright shining.

translations.literal: Liu you means the six directions of dark distance.
translations.idiomatic: The six you are the six reaches of shadow.

translations.literal: Gui qu means distant regions.
translations.idiomatic: Ghost lands are farthest borders.

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Gaozong campaigned against the Gui region."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi names the Gui Fang campaign.

translations.literal: Te means evil.
translations.idiomatic: Te is malice.

translations.literal: Jiong means far.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong is remoteness.

translations.literal: Min means to extinguish.
translations.idiomatic: Min is annihilation.

translations.literal: Suo means small.
translations.idiomatic: Suo means petty or small.

translations.literal: Yi means to nurture.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means to feed or foster.

sentences.zh: 言凶惡者無遠而不滅，微細者何小而不養也。

translations.literal: It means evildoers, however far, are not left undestroyed; however small the trifling Hu, they are not left unnurtured.
translations.idiomatic: The wicked perish however distant; the smallest tribes gain nurture however slight.

translations.literal: Note [three]: The three powers are Heaven, earth, and man.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: heaven, earth, humanity.

translations.literal: The Changes says: "It doubles the three powers and pairs them.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi doubles each of the three powers.

translations.literal: " Deng means to ascend.
translations.idiomatic: " Deng means rise.

translations.literal: Ji means merit.
translations.idiomatic: Ji is achievement.

sentences.zh: 言升天之功，非堯不能興也。

translations.literal: It means the merit of ascending to heaven — none but Yao could raise it.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven-ascending merit begins with Yao alone.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Brightly ascending on high."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu speaks of shining ascent.

translations.literal: " Pu means to spread.
translations.idiomatic: " Pu means to publish abroad.

sentences.zh: 遺策，堯之餘策，謂堯典也。

translations.literal: Yi ce means Yao leftover plans — referring to the Canon of Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Yi ce is the Yao dian legacy.

translations.literal: Zai xia means later generations of descendants.
translations.idiomatic: Those below are posterity.

sentences.zh: 言堯典為子孫之訓，非漢不能弘大也。

translations.literal: It means that the Canon of Yao as a teaching for descendants — none but Han could enlarge it.
translations.idiomatic: Only Han could magnify Yao lesson for heirs.

sentences.zh: 注[四]經緯天地，言陰陽交泰也。

translations.literal: Note [four]: Warping and wefting heaven and earth means yin and yang in perfect union.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: ordering heaven-earth is thriving yin-yang.

sentences.zh: 出入三光，言日、月、星得其度也。

translations.literal: Going forth and entering with the three luminaries means sun, moon, and stars keeping their measures.
translations.idiomatic: The three lights keep proper measure.

translations.literal: Hun yuan is the general name for heaven and earth.
translations.idiomatic: Hun yuan names the cosmos.

translations.literal: Hao mang means fine and minute.
translations.idiomatic: Hao mang is the finest grain.

sentences.zh: 老子曰：「和陰陽，節四時，潤乎草木，浸乎金石，毫毛潤澤。

translations.literal: Laozi says: "Harmonize yin and yang, regulate the four seasons, moisten grass and trees, steep metal and stone — down to the tip of a hair, all is sleek."
translations.idiomatic: Laozi runs from seasons to dew on metal.

translations.literal: " Xing means life.
translations.idiomatic: " Xing means living beings.

translations.literal: Xun means to follow.
translations.idiomatic: Xun means accord with pattern.

translations.literal: All that bear life follow the pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Every creature follows the norm.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「別生分類，品物萬殊。

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Distinguishing life, dividing classes — the myriad things in endless variety."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu sorts kinds into myriad forms.

translations.literal: " Heng means penetration.
translations.idiomatic: " Heng means thriving flow.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「含弘光大，品物鹹亨。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Embracing greatness, shining broadly — the myriad things all thrive."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi says breadth lets all things flourish.

translations.literal: How splendid!
translations.idiomatic: How glorious!

sentences.zh: 皇家帝世，德臣列辟，功君百王，榮鏡宇宙，尊無與抗。

translations.literal: The imperial house, age after age — in virtue it could subject the arrayed lords, in merit it could lord over the hundred kings; its glory mirrors the universe, its supremacy matches nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Dynasty after dynasty Han virtue could humble every ancient king, Han deeds outrank the hundred thrones, its splendor fills heaven and earth, and nothing rivals its height.

translations.literal: [One] Then it began firm and reverent, laboring in *
translations.idiomatic: [1] Then came firm reverence, laboring in

translations.literal: (rang)
translations.idiomatic: (yielding)

sentences.zh: **[謙]*，兢兢業業，貶成抑定，不敢論製作。

translations.literal: **modest* humility, fearful and cautious, belittling what was achieved and restraining what was settled, not daring to discuss making institutions.
translations.idiomatic: **humble** care, shrinking from claiming rites and music.

sentences.zh: [二]至令遷正黜色賓監之事煥揚宇內，而禮官儒林屯朋篤論之士而不傳祖宗之彷彿，雖雲優慎，無乃葸歟！

translations.literal: [Two] Down to now the affairs of changing the first month, altering the color scheme, guest status, and taking warning shine within the four seas, yet the masters of ritual and the clustered scholars of earnest debate do not transmit even the rough outline of the ancestors — though they call it cautious ease, is it not too timid!
translations.idiomatic: [2] Calendar and regalia shine, yet scholars never set the founders legacy to paper — too timid by half!

sentences.zh: [三] 注[一]皇家帝代謂漢家歷代也。

translations.literal: [Three] Note [one]: Huang jia di shi means the successive ages of the Han house.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Commentary [1]: imperial generations are Han lines.

translations.literal: Lie bi means the ancient emperors and kings.
translations.idiomatic: Lie bi are past sovereigns.

sentences.zh: 言漢家德可以臣彼列辟，功可以君彼百王。

translations.literal: It means that in virtue Han could make those lords its ministers, in merit it could be lord over those hundred kings.
translations.idiomatic: Han virtue could humble old kings, Han deeds could rule their annals.

sentences.zh: 相如封禪書曰：「歷選列辟。

translations.literal: Xiangru Feng and Shan memorial says: "Choosing in turn among the arrayed lords."
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru speaks of sorting the lords.

translations.literal: Jing is like brightness.
translations.idiomatic: Jing is mirror-bright glory.

translations.literal: Kang means match or rival; read like kang.
translations.idiomatic: Kang means peer; read kang.

translations.literal: Note [two] The Erya says: "Qian gong means firm."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: qian gong is steadfast.

translations.literal: " The Changes says: "The laboring and modest gentleman has an auspicious end."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi praises laboring modesty.

translations.literal: " It means the emperor was indeed laboring and modest.
translations.idiomatic: The sovereign truly practiced laboring modesty.

translations.literal: Jing jing means fearful caution.
translations.idiomatic: Jing jing is wary care.

translations.literal: Ye ye means fearful of peril.
translations.idiomatic: Ye ye is trembling vigilance.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「王者功成作樂，理定制禮。

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: "When the king merit is complete he makes music; when order is fixed he makes ritual."
translations.idiomatic: The Li ji ties music and rites to completed merit.

sentences.zh: 」今不敢論制禮作樂之事，言謙之甚也。

translations.literal: " Now not daring to discuss making ritual and music shows the extremity of modesty.
translations.idiomatic: Silence on rites and music shows deep humility.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Qian zheng means changing the first month of the year.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: calendar reform.

translations.literal: Chu se means changing the color of robes.
translations.idiomatic: Regalia colors changed with dynasty.

sentences.zh: 賓謂殷週二王之後，為漢之賓。

translations.literal: Bin means the two royal descendants of Yin and Zhou, who became guests of Han.
translations.idiomatic: Guests are Shang and Zhou heirs honored by Han.

translations.literal: Jian means to observe.
translations.idiomatic: Jian is to take warning.

translations.literal: Observing Yin and Zhou affairs to take as mirror and warning.
translations.idiomatic: Han mirrors Yin and Zhou precedent.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子曰：「周監於二代。

translations.literal: The Analects records Confucius as saying: "Zhou took warning from the two previous dynasties."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius says Zhou watched the two prior ages.

translations.literal: " Tun means to gather.
translations.idiomatic: " Tun means to cluster.

translations.literal: Peng means companions.
translations.idiomatic: Peng means peers or cohorts.

sentences.zh: 不傳謂不製作篇籍，以紀功德也。

translations.literal: Bu chuan means not composing texts to record merit and virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Bu chuan is failing to write the deeds down.

translations.literal: Fang fu means rough outline.
translations.idiomatic: Fang fu is a rough sketch.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子曰：「慎而無禮則葸。

translations.literal: The Analects records Confucius as saying: "If cautious but without ritual, then timid."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius warns caution without ritual turns cowardly.

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "Xi is a plain, dull appearance."
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: xi is blunt, timorous dullness.

sentences.zh: 言雖優遊謙慎，無乃太質□也。

translations.literal: It means that though leisurely and modestly cautious, is it not too plain and dull?
translations.idiomatic: Easy modesty risks sliding into blunt timidity.

sentences.zh: 於是三事岳牧之僚，僉爾而進曰：陛下仰監唐典，中述祖則，俯蹈宗軌。

translations.literal: Thereupon the colleagues of the three high ministers and the mountain shepherds all advanced together, saying: "Your Majesty looks up to mirror the Tang canons, in the middle narrates ancestral models, below treads the tracks of the temple line.
translations.idiomatic: Then dukes and shepherds advanced: you mirror Tang, tell ancestors, follow zong paths.

sentences.zh: [一] 躬奉天經，惇睦辯章之化洽。

translations.literal: [One] Personally upholding heaven constant, the transformation of generous harmony and clear distinction is thorough.
translations.idiomatic: [1] You hold filial piety as heaven norm; kinship and civic order spread wide.

translations.literal: [Two] Touring to pacify the black-haired masses, the kindness of cherishing and protecting widows and orphans penetrates everywhere.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Tours soothe the people; care reaches widows and orphans.

sentences.zh: [三]燔瘞縣沉，肅祗腢神之禮備。

translations.literal: [Three] Burning, burying, hanging, and sinking — the rites of reverent respect to the host of spirits are complete.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Every sacrifice mode is performed to the gods.

translations.literal: [Four] Therefore *
translations.idiomatic: [4] Therefore

translations.literal: (fenghuang)
translations.idiomatic: (phoenix)

sentences.zh: *來儀集羽族於觀魏，肉角馴毛宗於外囿，擾緇文皓質於郊，升黃暉采鱗於沼，甘露宵零於豐草，三足軒翥於茂樹。

translations.literal: * came in measured pace and gathered winged kind on the gate towers; horned flesh and docile hairy herds filled the outer parks; the black-striped white-bodied beast was tamed at the suburb; the yellow-gleaming scaly one rose in the pool; sweet dew fell by night on lush grass; three-footed birds soared among thick trees.
translations.idiomatic: phoenixes patterned the towers, unicorns the parks, zouyu at the border, yellow dragon in the pool, dew at night, three-legged crows in the trees.

sentences.zh: [五] 若乃嘉谷靈草，奇獸神禽，應圖合諜，窮祥極瑞者，朝夕□牧，日月邦畿，卓犖乎方州，羨溢乎要荒。

translations.literal: [Five] As for fine grain and numinous plants, strange beasts and divine birds that match charts and tally with annals, exhaustively auspicious and prodigious — morning and evening in the outskirts, day and month within the royal domain — they stand out in the provinces, overflow even to the farthest wilds.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Omen grain, spirit herbs, rare beasts fill capital and frontier.

sentences.zh: [六]昔姬有素雉、朱烏、玄秬、黃□之事耳，君臣動色，左右相趨，濟濟翼翼，峨峨如也。

translations.literal: [Six] Formerly the Ji house had only the white pheasant, red crow, black millet, and yellow barley — yet ruler and ministers changed color, left and right hurried along, stately and solemn, towering in awe.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Zhou had only white pheasant, red crow, black millet, and yellow barley, yet the whole court stood in awe.

sentences.zh: [七]蓋用昭明寅畏，承聿懷之福。

translations.literal: [Seven] Thus they displayed bright reverent awe and received the blessing of continued remembrance.
translations.idiomatic: [7] So they showed reverence and won lasting blessing.

sentences.zh: 亦以寵靈文武，貽燕後昆，覆以懿鑠，豈其為身而有顓辭也？

translations.literal: Also thereby honoring the spirits of Wen and Wu, leaving peace to later descendants, heaping fine praise — could they have had partial phrases for themselves alone?
translations.idiomatic: They blessed Wen and Wu spirits and heirs — not for private boast.

sentences.zh: [八]若然受之，宜亦勤恁旅力，以充厥道，啟恭館之金縢，御東序之秘寶，以流其占。

translations.literal: [Eight] If so in receiving it, you should likewise labor with all your strength to fill that way — open the metal coffer of the reverent hall, mount the secret treasures of the eastern gallery, and let their prognostications flow forth.
translations.idiomatic: [8] Accept omens boldly: open the archives, read the tortoise, publish the signs.

translations.literal: [Nine] Note [one]: The three affairs are the three dukes.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Commentary [1]: three affairs are three gong.

translations.literal: Qian means all.
translations.idiomatic: Qian means everyone.

translations.literal: Note [two]: Tian jing means filial piety.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: heaven constant is filial piety.

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Filial piety is heaven constant."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Filial Piety calls piety heaven norm.

sentences.zh: 」謂章帝初即位，四時禘祫，宗祀於明堂也。

translations.literal: " It refers to when Emperor Zhang first ascended the throne, performing the seasonal di and xia sacrifices, lineage sacrifice in the Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi opened his reign with Ming tang lineage rites each season.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Treat generously the nine kindreds."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu urges thick care for nine clans.

translations.literal: " It also says: "When the nine kindreds are harmonious, distinguish and clarify the hundred surnames."
translations.idiomatic: When kin are harmonious, clarify the hundred clans.

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan says: "Bian means to separate.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: bian is to sort.

translations.literal: Zhang means to clarify.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang is to make clear.

translations.literal: Dun means thick.
translations.idiomatic: Dun means deep kindness.

translations.literal: Mu means intimate.
translations.idiomatic: Mu means close kinship.

sentences.zh: 」章帝性篤愛，不忍與諸王乖離，皆留京師也。

translations.literal: " Emperor Zhang nature was deeply loving; he could not bear to part from the various kings — all were kept in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi kept princes at the capital from fondness.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Xun means to soothe.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: xun is to pacify on tour.

translations.literal: Jing means to pacify.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means to settle.

translations.literal: Li, zheng — both mean the multitude.
translations.idiomatic: Li and zheng mean the common people.

translations.literal: Huai means to cherish.
translations.idiomatic: Huai is thoughtful care.

translations.literal: Bao means to secure.
translations.idiomatic: Bao is to keep safe.

translations.literal: Jia means to pervade.
translations.idiomatic: Jia means reaching everywhere.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「懷保小人，惠鮮□寡。

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Cherish and secure the little people; show kindness to widows and orphans."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu tells kings to feed widows and orphans.

sentences.zh: 」謂章帝在位凡四巡狩，賜人爵，□、寡、孤、獨不能自存者粟。

translations.literal: " It refers to Emperor Zhang four times on tour, granting noble ranks, giving grain to widowers, widows, orphans, and those unable to support themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi toured four times, ennobling men, feeding widowers, widows, orphans, and the destitute.

sentences.zh: 注[四]爾雅曰：「祭天曰燔柴，祭地曰瘞埋，祭山曰庋縣，祭川曰浮沉。

translations.literal: Note [four] The Erya says: "To sacrifice to heaven is called burning brushwood; to earth, burying; to mountains, suspending; to rivers, sinking and floating."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: Erya sacrifice names for heaven, earth, hill, river.

translations.literal: " Su zhi means reverent respect.
translations.idiomatic: Su zhi is grave reverence.

sentences.zh: 封禪書曰：「湯武至尊，不失肅敬。

translations.literal: The Feng and Shan memorial says: "Tang and Wu were most honored yet did not lose reverent awe."
translations.idiomatic: Even Tang and Wu stayed reverent.

sentences.zh: 」元和中詔曰：「朕巡狩岱宗，柴望山川。

translations.literal: In the Yuanhe edict it said: "When We toured to venerate Mount Dai, We burned brushwood and gazed on mountains and rivers."
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe edict: imperial tour to Tai, burning sacrifice.

translations.literal: Gui is read ju-hui fan.
translations.idiomatic: Gui: fanqie ju-hui.

translations.literal: Note [five] The Documents says: "The phoenix came in measured pace."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: Shang shu phoenix line.

sentences.zh: 」元和二年詔曰：「乃者鳳皇鸞鳥比集七郡。

translations.literal: " The Yuanhe 2 edict said: "Recently phoenixes and simurghs have gathered in succession in seven commanderies."
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 2: phoenixes flocked in seven jurisdictions.

translations.literal: Yu zu means the many birds that followed them.
translations.idiomatic: Other birds followed the phoenixes.

translations.literal: Guan wei means gate towers.
translations.idiomatic: Guan wei are palace gate towers.

translations.literal: Rou jiao means the unicorn.
translations.idiomatic: Rou jiao is the horned unicorn.

sentences.zh: 伏侯古今注曰：「建初二年，北海得一角獸，大如□，有角在耳閒，端有肉。

translations.literal: The Fu Hou ancient and modern notes say: "In Jianchu 2 Beihai obtained a one-horned beast as large as a pony, with a horn between the ears, fleshy tip.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 2: Beihai reported a unicorn the size of a pony, horn between the ears.

sentences.zh: 又元和二年，麒麟見陳，一角，端如蔥葉，色赤黃。

translations.literal: Also in Yuanhe 2 a unicorn appeared in Chen, one horn, tip like scallion leaf, color red-yellow."
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 2: unicorn in Chen, scallion-shaped horn.

translations.literal: " Rao means tame.
translations.idiomatic: " Rao means domesticated.

translations.literal: Zi wen hao zhi means the zouyu.
translations.idiomatic: Black stripes on white hide is the zouyu.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「騶虞，白虎，黑文，尾長於身。

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: "Zouyu — white tiger, black markings, tail longer than its body.
translations.idiomatic: The Shuowen defines zouyu as a white tiger with black stripes and long tail.

sentences.zh: 」古今注曰：「元和三年，白虎見彭城。

translations.literal: " Ancient and modern notes say: "In Yuanhe 3 a white tiger appeared in Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 3: white tiger at Pengcheng.

translations.literal: " Yellow gleam and scaly form means the yellow dragon.
translations.idiomatic: Yellow dragon omen.

sentences.zh: 建初五年，有八黃龍見於零陵。

translations.literal: In Jianchu 5 eight yellow dragons appeared in Lingling.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 5: eight yellow dragons at Lingling.

sentences.zh: 古今注曰：「元和二年，甘露降河南，三足烏集沛國。

translations.literal: " Ancient and modern notes say: "In Yuanhe 2 sweet dew fell on Henan; three-footed crows gathered in Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 2: dew in Henan, three-legged crows in Pei.

translations.literal: " Xuan zhu means flying up and down.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan zhu is wheeling flight.

translations.literal: Note [six]: Jia gu means fine grain — auspicious ears.
translations.idiomatic: Fine grain means auspicious rice.

translations.literal: Ling cao means the mushroom kind.
translations.idiomatic: Spirit herbs are ganoderma types.

sentences.zh: 古今注曰：「元和二年，芝生沛，如人冠大，坐狀。

translations.literal: " Ancient and modern notes say: "In Yuanhe 2 a fungus grew in Pei as large as a cap, in sitting posture.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 2: giant fungus at Pei.

sentences.zh: 」章和九年詔曰：「嘉谷滋生，芝草之類，歲月不絕。

translations.literal: " The Zhanghe 9 edict said: "Fine grain keeps sprouting; fungi never cease year to year.
translations.idiomatic: Zhanghe 9 edict on ceaseless grain and fungi.

sentences.zh: 」奇獸神禽謂白虎白雉之屬也。

translations.literal: " Strange beasts and divine birds mean white tiger, white pheasant, and the like.
translations.idiomatic: Rare beasts include white tiger and pheasant.

translations.literal: In Jianchu 7 a white deer was captured.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 7: white deer taken.

sentences.zh: 元和元年，日南獻生犀、白雉。

translations.literal: In Yuanhe 1 Rinan presented a live rhinoceros and white pheasants.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 1: Rinan sent rhino and white pheasants.

sentences.zh: 言應於瑞圖，又合於史諜也。

translations.literal: It says they answered the auspicious charts and matched the historiographers records.
translations.idiomatic: They matched omens and annals.

translations.literal: Jiao mu means the suburban wilds.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao mu is the capital outskirts.

translations.literal: Zhuo luo means extraordinary.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo luo means outstanding.

translations.literal: Xian is read yi-zhan fan.
translations.idiomatic: Xian: fanqie yi-zhan.

sentences.zh: 注[七]孝經援神契曰：「周成王時，越裳來獻白雉。

translations.literal: Note [seven] The Classic of Filial Piety, Assorted Proofs of Spirit says: "In King Cheng of Zhou time Yuechang came to present a white pheasant.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan shen qi: Yuechang pheasant under Cheng.

translations.literal: " Zhu wu means the red crow.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu wu is the crimson crow.

sentences.zh: 尚書中候曰：「太子發度孟津，有火自天止於王屋，流為赤烏。

translations.literal: The Documents, Central Proofs says: "When crown prince Fa crossed Meng Ford, fire came from heaven and stopped on the king roof, then flowed to become a red crow.
translations.idiomatic: Shang shu zhong hou: red crow at Mengjin.

translations.literal: " Xuan ju is black millet.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan ju is black glutinous millet.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「誕降嘉種，惟秬惟秠。

translations.literal: The Greater Ya says: "Heaven sent down fine seed — ju and pi.
translations.idiomatic: The Greater Ya praises fine grain.

translations.literal: " Yellow □ means wheat.
translations.idiomatic: Yellow grain here is wheat.

translations.literal: It means the red crow brought malt in its beak.
translations.idiomatic: The crow brought barley stalks.

translations.literal: The hymn says: "They left us the coming barley.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou song mentions barley gift.

translations.literal: " The Greater Ya says: "So many officers, stately.
translations.idiomatic: The Greater Ya praises many ministers.

translations.literal: " It also says: "Only this King Wen — careful and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Wen is called cautious.

translations.literal: " It also says: "They bore the jade libation cups, towering."
translations.idiomatic: Bear jade cups aloft.

sentences.zh: 注[八]詩大雅曰：「昭明有融。

translations.literal: Note [eight] The Greater Ya says: "Bright and merging radiance.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [8]: bright merging light.

translations.literal: " Yin means reverent.
translations.idiomatic: Yin is grave respect.

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Solemn, respectful, reverently awed.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu stresses solemn awe.

translations.literal: " Yu means to continue.
translations.idiomatic: Yu means to carry on.

translations.literal: Huai means to think.
translations.idiomatic: Huai is mindful care.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「昭事上帝，聿懷多福。

translations.literal: The Greater Ya says: "Brightly serving High God, thereby embracing many blessings.
translations.idiomatic: Serve God to win blessing.

translations.literal: " Yi means to leave behind.
translations.idiomatic: Yi means bequeath.

translations.literal: Yan means peace.
translations.idiomatic: Yan is repose.

translations.literal: Hou kun means descendants.
translations.idiomatic: Hou kun are later heirs.

sentences.zh: 言此並以光寵神靈文王、武王之德，遺燕安於子孫也。

translations.literal: It means all this together brought glory to the spirits of Wen and Wu, leaving peace to descendants.
translations.idiomatic: These acts honored Wen and Wu and left peace to heirs.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「貽厥孫謀，以燕翼子。

translations.literal: The Greater Ya says: "He left plans to his grandson, to give wing to his sons.
translations.idiomatic: The Greater Ya on leaving plans for children.

translations.literal: " Fu means again or layered.
translations.idiomatic: Fu means heaped or double.

translations.literal: Yi and shuo both mean beautiful.
translations.idiomatic: Yi and shuo both mean fine praise.

translations.literal: The Greater Ya says: "We sought fine virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Seeking fine virtue.

translations.literal: " It also says: "Oh, how splendid the royal army."
translations.idiomatic: Praising the royal host.

sentences.zh: 」言詩人歌頌周之盛德，當成康之時。

translations.literal: It means the poets sang the great virtue of Zhou at the height of Cheng and Kang.
translations.idiomatic: Poets hymned Zhou at Cheng and Kang height.

sentences.zh: 其成王、康王，豈獨為身而有自專之辭也，並上寵文武之業，下遺子孫之基也。

translations.literal: Did Cheng and Kang have selfish phrases for themselves alone? They above honored Wen and Wu enterprise, below bequeathed foundation to sons and grandsons.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng and Kang did not boast for self; they exalted ancestors and heirs.

sentences.zh: 言今章帝既獲符瑞之應，亦宜同成康之事也。

translations.literal: It means that now Emperor Zhang, having obtained these sign responses, ought likewise to act as Cheng and Kang did.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi with such omens should follow Cheng and Kang.

sentences.zh: 注[九]受之謂漢受此符瑞也。

translations.literal: Note [nine]: "Receiving it" means Han receiving these omens.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [9]: accepting the portents means Han.

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: "Ren means to think.
translations.idiomatic: Shuowen: ren is to ponder.

translations.literal: " Read ren-shen fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie ren-shen.

translations.literal: Lu means to array.
translations.idiomatic: Lu means to set forth.

translations.literal: Chong means to match or fill.
translations.idiomatic: Chong means to meet the charge.

translations.literal: The hall of reverent awe means within the temple.
translations.idiomatic: The reverent hall is the ancestral shrine.

sentences.zh: 金縢，以金緘匱，藏符瑞之書於其中也。

translations.literal: The metal coffer — binding the chest with metal — stores the books of omens inside.
translations.idiomatic: The metal-bound coffer held omen documents.

translations.literal: Yu means to display.
translations.idiomatic: Yu means to lay out.

translations.literal: The eastern gallery is the eastern wing.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern sequence is the east wing.

translations.literal: Mi bao means items like the River Chart.
translations.idiomatic: Secret treasures include the Hetu.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「天球河圖在東序。

translations.literal: The Documents says: "The celestial sphere and River Chart are in the eastern gallery.
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu places the chart in the east hall.

translations.literal: " Kong Anguo commentary says: "The River Chart is the eight trigrams.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo: Hetu is the eight trigrams.

sentences.zh: 」言啟金縢之書及河圖之卦以佔之也。

translations.literal: " It means opening the metal coffer writings and the River Chart hexagrams to divine by them.
translations.idiomatic: Open coffer and chart to divine.

translations.literal: Liu means perhaps the same as display.
translations.idiomatic: Liu here means to pour forth or apply.

translations.literal: Charts and writings are bright and manifest — heaven wisdom;
translations.idiomatic: Charts and books shine with heaven wit;

translations.literal: Kong You first command — sage trust;
translations.idiomatic: Kong plan foreordained — sage faith;

translations.literal: embodying practice, virtue root — upright nature;
translations.idiomatic: acting the root of virtue — true nature;

translations.literal: meeting good on the chosen day — great mandate.
translations.idiomatic: meeting lucky hour — the great charge.

sentences.zh: [一]順命以創製，定性以和神，荅三靈之繁祉，展放唐之明文，茲事體大而允，寤寐次於聖心。

translations.literal: [One] Following the charge to create institutions, fixing nature to harmonize spirits, answering the triple numen thick blessing, unfolding Tang Yao bright pattern — this affair is great and trustworthy; waking and sleeping it rests in the sage heart.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Obey heaven to set rites, harmonize gods, answer three powers, model Yao — vast, sure, ever in the sage mind.

sentences.zh: 瞻前顧後，豈蔑清廟憚□天乎？

translations.literal: Looking before and behind — would you slight the clear temple and shrink from correcting heaven mandate?
translations.idiomatic: Would you scorn the temple and fear heaven mandate?

sentences.zh: [二]伊考自邃古，乃降戾爰茲，作者七十有四人，有不俾而假素，罔光度而遺章，今其如台而獨闕也！

translations.literal: [Two] Examining from deepest antiquity down to this age — the performers were seventy-four; some possessed the realm yet did not enjoin fengshan, only feigned it on bamboo and silk; none who glorified the law and left their writings failed to fengshan — how can you alone leave a gap!
translations.idiomatic: [2] Since antiquity seventy-four rulers fengshan; none who could glorify the code skipped it — why alone omit it?

sentences.zh: [三] 注[一]圖書，河圖、洛書也。

translations.literal: [Three] Note [one]: Charts and writings are Hetu and Luoshu.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Commentary [1]: charts and writings are river and Luo books.

translations.literal: Liang means trust.
translations.idiomatic: Liang is faithful.

translations.literal: Zhang means manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang is clear.

translations.literal: Zhe means wise.
translations.idiomatic: Zhe is sagely wisdom.

sentences.zh: 言天授圖書者，為天子所知也。

translations.literal: It means heaven granting charts and writings is known to the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven books are revealed to the sovereign.

translations.literal: Kong.
translations.idiomatic: Kong:

translations.literal: Kong means Kong Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: Kong is Confucius.

translations.literal: You means plan.
translations.idiomatic: You is a charted plan.

translations.literal: Fu means trust.
translations.idiomatic: Fu is reliability.

sentences.zh: 言孔丘之圖，先命漢家當須封禪，此聖人之信也。

translations.literal: It means the chart of Kong Qiu beforehand commanded the Han house must perform feng and shan — this is the sage trust.
translations.idiomatic: Confucian prophecy fixed Han fengshan as sage pledge.

translations.literal: Ti xing means personally practicing.
translations.idiomatic: Ti xing is to live the teaching.

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Filial piety is the root of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao jing: filial piety is virtue root.

translations.literal: " The Changes says: "The qian way changes and transforms; each corrects its nature and life.
translations.idiomatic: The Yi says qian transforms each nature.

translations.literal: " Ding means matching.
translations.idiomatic: Ding means to meet.

translations.literal: Chen means the time.
translations.idiomatic: Chen is the timely hour.

translations.literal: Jing means great.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means great mandate.

sentences.zh: 逢休吉之代，當封禪之時，此為天子之大命也。

translations.literal: Meeting a blessed age, facing the fengshan season — this is the Son of Heaven great charge.
translations.idiomatic: Good age plus fengshan moment is the sovereign great destiny.

translations.literal: Note [two]: Ming means the omens.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: charge means portents.

translations.literal: Da means to answer.
translations.idiomatic: Da is to respond.

translations.literal: San ling are the spirits of heaven, earth, and man.
translations.idiomatic: The three numina are heaven, earth, man.

translations.literal: Fan means many.
translations.idiomatic: Fan means abundant.

translations.literal: Zhi means blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi is good fortune.

translations.literal: Zhan means to spread.
translations.idiomatic: Zhan is to display.

translations.literal: Fang means to imitate; read fu-wang fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fang is to model; fanqie fu-wang.

translations.literal: Imitating Yao Tang pattern means feng and shan.
translations.idiomatic: Modeling Yao is the fengshan rite.

sentences.zh: 尚書琁璣鈐曰：「平制禮樂，於唐之文。

translations.literal: The Documents, Xuan ji qian says: "He balanced ritual and music according to Tang pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Apocryphon on Tang-pattern ritual.

translations.literal: " This affair means fengshan — great and trustworthy.
translations.idiomatic: The matter is fengshan: grand and sure.

translations.literal: Ci means to stop.
translations.idiomatic: Ci means to lodge or rest.

sentences.zh: 寤寐常止於聖心，言不可忘之也。

translations.literal: Waking and sleeping it always stops in the sage heart — meaning it cannot be forgotten.
translations.idiomatic: It never leaves the sage waking or sleeping.

sentences.zh: 前謂前代帝王，後謂子孫也。

translations.literal: Qian means former dynasties emperors; hou means descendants.
translations.idiomatic: Before is past kings; after is posterity.

translations.literal: Mie means to slight.
translations.idiomatic: Mie is to treat lightly.

translations.literal: Dan means difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: Dan is to shrink from difficulty.

translations.literal: □ means to correct.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss means to set heaven mandate aright.

sentences.zh: 言封禪之事，皆述祖宗之德，今乃推讓，豈輕清廟而難正天命乎？

translations.literal: It means the fengshan affair always narrates ancestral virtue; now you defer — would you slight the clear temple and shrink from correcting heaven mandate?
translations.idiomatic: Fengshan hymns ancestors; to defer is to slight the temple and dodge heaven charge.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「□天之命，惟時惟幾。」

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Revere Heaven mandate — only seize the moment, only watch the danger."
translations.idiomatic: The Shang shu urges holding Heaven mandate in the nick of time.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Yi means "indeed."
translations.idiomatic: Yi is the particle "indeed."

translations.literal: Sui gu is like high antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Sui gu means farthest antiquity.

translations.literal: The Songs of Chu says: "At the beginning of deep antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Chu ci opens with deep antiquity.

translations.literal: " Li means to arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Li means reaching here.

sentences.zh: 言自遠古以來至於此也。

translations.literal: It means from remote antiquity down to this time.
translations.idiomatic: From remotest ages to now.

translations.literal: Zuo zhe means those who performed fengshan.
translations.idiomatic: Performers are fengshan rulers.

sentences.zh: 史記管仲曰：「自古封禪七十二君。

translations.literal: The Records, Guan Zhong says: "From antiquity there have been seventy-two lords who fengshan."
translations.idiomatic: Guan Zhong counts seventy-two fengshan lords.

translations.literal: " Counting Wudi and Guangwu makes seventy-four lords.
translations.idiomatic: Adding Han Wudi and Guangwu yields seventy-four.

translations.literal: Bi means to cause.
translations.idiomatic: Bi means to make someone act.

sentences.zh: 有天下不使其封禪，而假為竹素之文者，無有光揚法度而□其文章，不封禪者也。

translations.literal: Those who possessed the realm yet did not have them perform fengshan, but only counterfeited it in writings on bamboo and silk; none who glorified the statutes and completed their texts failed to fengshan —
translations.idiomatic: Some kings faked fengshan on paper; none who truly spread the law skipped the rite.

translations.literal: Tai means "I."
translations.idiomatic: Tai is the pronoun "I."

translations.literal: Now how can you alone leave a gap!
translations.idiomatic: How can we alone stand aside?

sentences.zh: 是時聖上固已垂精游神，包舉蓺文，屢訪腢儒，諭咨故老，與之乎斟酌道德之淵源，餚核仁義之林藪，以望元符之臻焉。

translations.literal: At this time the sage sovereign had already let his essence roam, embraced all literary arts, repeatedly consulted the host of scholars, instructed and questioned the old men, with them weighing the deep springs of the Way and its virtue, tasting the thickets of benevolence and righteousness, hoping for the arrival of the great omen.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangdi already ranged his mind over learning, asked elders, tasted the Way, and awaited the great portent.

sentences.zh: [一]既成腢後之讜辭，又悉經五繇之碩慮矣。

translations.literal: [One] Having completed the straight counsel of the host of ministers, and fully sounded the great thoughts of the five oracle texts,
translations.idiomatic: [1] Ministers had spoken plain truth; the five divination threads were weighed.

sentences.zh: 將絣萬嗣，煬洪暉，奮景炎，扇遺風，播芳烈，久而愈新，用而不竭，汪汪乎丕天之大律，其疇能□之哉？

translations.literal: he will continue myriad heirs, blaze great radiance, raise blazing fire, fan the wind left behind, spread fragrant deeds — ever newer with use, never exhausted — how deep the great pattern of heaven; who can exhaust it?
translations.idiomatic: He will pass glory to heirs, fan old virtue, burn bright — deep as heaven law; who can plumb it?

translations.literal: How Tang! How august! How august! How Tang!
translations.idiomatic: Tang splendor! Han splendor! Han splendor! Tang splendor!

translations.literal: [Two] Note [one]: Sheng shang means Emperor Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Commentary [1]: the sage above is Zhangdi.

translations.literal: Yu means to tell;
translations.idiomatic: Yu is to instruct;

translations.literal: zi means to plan.
translations.idiomatic: zi is to consult plans.

sentences.zh: 道德仁義，人所常行，故以酒食為諭焉。

translations.literal: The Way, virtue, benevolence, and righteousness are what people constantly practice; therefore wine and food are used as metaphors.
translations.idiomatic: Morals are likened to a feast.

translations.literal: Yuan yuan and lin sou metaphor depth.
translations.idiomatic: Springs and thickets image depth.

translations.literal: Yuan means heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan is heaven.

translations.literal: Fu means omen.
translations.idiomatic: Fu is a portent.

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Dishes and kernels were many.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes line on many dishes.

translations.literal: " He also means kernel — referring to fruits and the like.
translations.idiomatic: He means fruit kernels.

translations.literal: Note [two]: Dang means straight speech.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: dang is candid words.

translations.literal: Yao means oracle text; read zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Yao is oracle wording; read zhou.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「先王卜征五年而歲習其祥，不習則增修其德而改卜。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: "Former kings divined campaigns for five years and each year rehearsed the omens; if they did not match, they increased their virtue and divined again."
translations.idiomatic: Zuo zhuan on five-year campaign divination.

translations.literal: " Shuo means great.
translations.idiomatic: Shuo means large-scale.

translations.literal: Lu means thought.
translations.idiomatic: Lu is deliberation.

sentences.zh: 廣雅曰：「絣，續也，音方萌反。

translations.literal: The Guang ya says: "Bing means to continue; read fang-meng fan.
translations.idiomatic: Guang ya: bing continues; fanqie fang-meng.

translations.literal: " Jing means great.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means vast.

translations.literal: Yan means the power of fire.
translations.idiomatic: Yan is fire virtue blaze.

translations.literal: Wang wang is like deep.
translations.idiomatic: Wang wang is abysmally deep.

sentences.zh: 今文尚書太誓篇曰：「立功立事，可以永年，丕天之大律。

translations.literal: The modern-text Documents, Tai shi chapter says: "Establish deeds and affairs, then the years can be long — the great pattern of heaven."
translations.idiomatic: Jin wen Tai shi praises heaven great norm.

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary says: "Pi means great.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: pi is great.

translations.literal: Lu means law.
translations.idiomatic: Lu is the pattern or law.

translations.literal: " Chou means who.
translations.idiomatic: Chou asks "who."

translations.literal: □ means "exhaust."
translations.idiomatic: The gloss means to exhaust or finish.

translations.literal: Tang zai means Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Tang zai acclaims Yao.

translations.literal: Huang zai means Han.
translations.idiomatic: Huang zai acclaims Han.

translations.literal: It means only Tang and Han, only Han and Tang.
translations.idiomatic: Only Yao age and Han match each other.

translations.literal: Later Gu left office on mourning for his mother.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu later resigned for his mother funeral.

sentences.zh: 永元初，大將軍竇憲出征匈奴，以固為中護軍，與參議。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Yongyuan, the great general Dou Xian marched against the Xiongnu and appointed Gu as central protector of the army to join counsel.
translations.idiomatic: Yongyuan opened with Dou Xian naming Ban Gu central hu jun.

sentences.zh: 北單于聞漢軍出，遣使款居延塞，欲修呼韓邪故事，朝見天子，請大使。

translations.literal: When the Northern Chanyu heard the Han army was coming, he sent envoys to treat at the Juyan barrier, wishing to renew the old story of Huhanye, court the Son of Heaven, and beg a great ambassador.
translations.idiomatic: The northern Shanyu asked for parity with Huhanye and a chief envoy.

sentences.zh: 憲上遣固行中郎將事，將數百騎與虜使俱出居延塞迎之。

translations.literal: Xian submitted that Gu should act as gentleman of the palace, leading several hundred riders to go out Juyan with the barbarian envoys to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian sent Ban Gu as zhonglang jiang to meet them at Juyan.

sentences.zh: 會南匈奴掩破北庭，[一]固至私渠海，聞虜中亂，引還。

translations.literal: It happened that the Southern Xiongnu suddenly crushed the northern court, [one] Gu reached Siquhai, heard turmoil among the barbarians, and led his force back.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Southern Xiongnu smashed the north; Ban Gu turned back at Siquhai.

translations.literal: When Dou Xian fell, Gu was first implicated and dismissed from office.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou fell, Ban Gu was stripped of rank first.

sentences.zh: 注[一]永元二年，南單于出雞鹿塞擊北匈奴於河雲，大破之。

translations.literal: Note [one]: In Yongyuan 2 the Southern Chanyu went out Jimu barrier and struck the Northern Xiongnu at Heyun, crushing them.
translations.idiomatic: Yongyuan 2: southern raid at Heyun.

sentences.zh: 固不教學諸子，諸子多不遵法度，吏人苦之。

translations.literal: Gu did not teach his sons; the sons mostly failed to observe the laws, and clerks suffered for it.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu neglected his sons discipline; officials suffered.

sentences.zh: 初，洛陽令種兢嘗行，固奴干其車騎，吏椎呼之，奴醉罵，兢大怒，畏憲不敢發，心銜之。

translations.literal: At first, when Luoyang magistrate Chong Jing was on rounds, Gu slave obstructed his carriage escort; clerks struck and shouted at the slave; the slave, drunk, cursed; Jing was furious but feared Dou Xian and did not act, yet harbored resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Chong Jing was insulted by Ban slave but feared Dou Xian.

sentences.zh: 及竇氏賓客皆逮考，兢因此捕系固，遂死獄中。

translations.literal: When all Dou guests were arrested and examined, Jing therefore seized Gu and bound him; he died in prison.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou fell, Chong Jing jailed Ban Gu, who died there.

translations.literal: At the time he was sixty-one sui.
translations.idiomatic: He was sixty-one.

sentences.zh: 詔以譴責兢，抵主者吏罪。

translations.literal: An edict rebuked Jing and punished the responsible clerks.
translations.idiomatic: The court rebuked Chong Jing and punished clerks.

sentences.zh: 固所著典引、賓戲、應譏、詩、賦、銘、誄、頌、書、文、記、論、議、六言，在者凡四十一篇。

translations.literal: Among Gu writings — Dian yin, Ke xi, Ying ji, poems, fu, inscriptions, dirges, eulogies, letters, essays, records, treatises, discussions, six-character verse — those extant number forty-one pieces in all.
translations.idiomatic: Forty-one of his works survive.

sentences.zh: 論曰：司馬遷、班固父子，其言史官載籍之作，大義粲然著矣。

translations.literal: Disquisition: Sima Qian and Ban Biao and Ban Gu, father and son — their words on the historian craft and what it records make the great meaning splendidly clear.
translations.idiomatic: The essay says Sima Qian and the Ban family clarified historiography.

sentences.zh: 議者咸稱二子有良史之才。

translations.literal: Critics all called the two sons men of fine historian talent.
translations.idiomatic: Critics praised both as master historians.

sentences.zh: 遷文直而事核，固文贍而事詳。

translations.literal: Qian prose is straight and events are compact; Gu prose is ample and events are detailed.
translations.idiomatic: Sima is lean and true; Ban is rich and full.

sentences.zh: 若固之序事，不激詭，不抑抗，[一] 贍而不穢，詳而有體，使讀之者亹亹而不猒，信哉其能成名也。

translations.literal: As for Gu ordering of events — not inciting, not destructive, not deflating, not inflating, [one] ample yet not foul, detailed yet structured, so that readers grow eager and never weary — truly he could win a name.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Ban narrative stays even, rich but clean, detailed yet shaped — readers never tire.

sentences.zh: [二]彪、固譏遷，以為是非頗謬於聖人。

translations.literal: [Two] Biao and Gu criticized Qian, thinking his judgments often strayed from the sages.
translations.idiomatic: [2] The Bans faulted Sima for straying from Confucius.

sentences.zh: [三]然其論議常排死節，否正直，而不廝殺身成仁之為美，[四]則輕仁義，賤守節愈矣。

translations.literal: [Three] Yet in their own discussions they often rejected dying for principle, denied uprightness, and did not quite treat dying to complete humanity as beautiful, [four] which goes even further toward slighting benevolence and righteousness and despising fidelity.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Yet they slighted martyrdom and upright death worse than Sima.

sentences.zh: [五]固傷遷博物洽聞，不能以智免極刑；

translations.literal: [Five] Gu pitied Qian broad learning and comprehensive hearing, yet being unable by wit to escape extreme punishment;
translations.idiomatic: [5] Ban pitied Sima learning yet suffering mutilation;

sentences.zh: [六]然亦身陷大戮，[七]智及之而不能守之。
