translations.literal: [six] yet he himself also fell into great execution, [seven] wisdom reached the point yet could not guard himself.
translations.idiomatic: [6] yet Ban too died violently, [7] wise yet unable to save himself.

sentences.zh: [八]嗚呼，古人所以致論於目睫也！

translations.literal: [eight] Alas — this is why the ancients made the eyelash parable!
translations.idiomatic: [8] Hence the ancients spoke of lashes and eyes.

translations.literal: [nine]
translations.idiomatic: Ninth note marker closing the disquisition.

translations.literal: Note [one]: Ji means to raise up.
translations.idiomatic: Ji is to heighten praise.

translations.literal: Gui means to tear down.
translations.idiomatic: Gui is to tear down.

translations.literal: Yi means to push down.
translations.idiomatic: Yi is to suppress.

translations.literal: Kang means to push up.
translations.idiomatic: Kang is to elevate.

sentences.zh: 注[二]爾雅曰，亹亹猶勉也。

translations.literal: Note [two] The Erya says: "Wei wei means striving."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: wei wei is tireless reading.

sentences.zh: 注[三]言遷所是非皆與聖人乖謬，即崇黃老而薄五經，輕仁義而賤守節是也。

translations.literal: Note [three] It means Qian judgments all diverged from the sages — honoring Huang-Lao while slighting the Five Classics, slighting benevolence and righteousness while despising holding to integrity — these sorts of things.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Sima favored Daoism, slighted Confucian virtue.

sentences.zh: 注[四]固序遊俠傳曰：「劇孟、郭解之徒，馳騖於閭閻，雖其陷於刑辟，自與殺身成名，若季路、仇牧*[死]*而不悔也。

translations.literal: Note [four] Gu preface to the wandering knights says: "Ji Meng, Guo Jie, and their sort galloped through lanes and alleys; though they fell into punishment, they themselves compared to dying for a name like Jilu or Qiu Mu *[dying]* without regret.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: Ban preface compares bravoes to martyrs.

sentences.zh: 古之正法：五伯，三王之罪人；

translations.literal: The correct law of antiquity: the Five Hegemons were criminals against the Three Kings;
translations.idiomatic: Ancient law: hegemons sinned against kings;

translations.literal: the six states were criminals against the Five Hegemons;
translations.idiomatic: Warring states sinned against hegemons;

translations.literal: the four heroes were again criminals against the six states.
translations.idiomatic: Four bravoes sinned against the states.

sentences.zh: 況於郭解之倫，以匹夫之細，竊殺生之權，其罪不容於誅也。」

translations.literal: How much more men like Guo Jie, in the pettiness of commoners, usurping the power over life and death — their guilt did not fit within execution."
translations.idiomatic: Petty bravoes seizing kill-power deserved death.

translations.literal: Note [five]: Yu means "even more."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: yu is "still more."

translations.literal: Note [six]: Meaning castration in the silkworm chamber.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: mutilation in silkworm house.

sentences.zh: 注[七]此已上略華嶠之辭。

translations.literal: Note [seven]: The above slightly summarizes Hua Qiao wording.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: paraphrases Hua Qiao.

translations.literal: Note [eight]: A saying of Confucius in the Analects.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [8]: Analects saying.

sentences.zh: 言有智而不能自守其身。

translations.literal: It means having wisdom yet being unable to guard one own person.
translations.idiomatic: Wit without self-preservation.

sentences.zh: 注[九]史記齊使者至越，曰：「幸也越之不亡也。

translations.literal: Note [nine] The Records: when the Qi envoy reached Yue he said: "It is fortunate that Yue is not yet destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [9]: Qi envoy eyelash parable.

sentences.zh: 吾不貴其智之如目，見豪毛而不見其睫也。

translations.literal: I do not prize wisdom like eyes that see an eyelash yet miss their own lashes.
translations.idiomatic: Eyes see lashes on others, not own lashes.

sentences.zh: 今越王知晉之失計，不自知越人之過，是目論也。

translations.literal: Now the king of Yue knows Jin miscalculation yet does not know the faults of his own Yue people — this is eye-only theory."
translations.idiomatic: Yue sees Jin error, not own fault.

sentences.zh: 」言班固譏遷被刑，而不知身自遇禍。

translations.literal: " It means Ban Gu criticized Qian for suffering mutilation yet did not know he would meet disaster himself.
translations.idiomatic: Ban mocked Sima fate yet shared it.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：二班懷文，裁成帝墳。

translations.literal: Encomium: The two Ban cherished letters and trimmed the emperor canon.
translations.idiomatic: The eulogy: both Bans shaped the imperial archive.

translations.literal: [One] Matching the excellence of Qian and Dong,
translations.idiomatic: [1] In stature they match Sima Qian and Dong Hu;

translations.literal: [two] also rivaling Qing and Yun.
translations.idiomatic: [2] in eloquence they rival Sima Xiangru and Yang Xiong.

sentences.zh: 注[一]沈約宋書曰：「初，謝儼作此贊，云『裁成典墳』，以示範曄，曄改為『帝墳』。」

translations.literal: [three] Biao knew the royal charge; Gu was lost in the age confusion.
translations.idiomatic: [3] Biao read the royal mandate; Ban Gu drowned in the intrigues of his day.

translations.literal: Note [one] Shen Yue Song shu says: "At first Xie Yan wrote this encomium saying trim the canonical tombs and showed it to Fan Ye; Ye changed it to emperor tombs."
translations.idiomatic: Fan Ye altered Xie Yan line from dian to di tombs.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「董狐，古之良史也。」

translations.literal: Note [two]: Meaning Sima Qian and Dong Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Qian and Dong Hu.

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: "Dong Hu was a fine historian of antiquity."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo zhuan calls Dong Hu a model scribe.

translations.literal: Note [three]: Sima Xiangru and Master Yang Yun.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Xiangru and Yang Xiong.
